# Ō¢ČŌ¢Č NEXT SESSION STARTS HERE ŌĆö **Glossary plan-and-execute (Phase 1) SHIPPED** ┬Ę branch `feat/composition-service` ┬Ę HEAD `658a0caa` ┬Ę 2026-06-25

> **WHAT SHIPPED:** the glossary assistant can now do **multi-step ontology goals in ONE confirm** ŌĆö the agent calls `glossary_plan(book_id, goal)`, a capable model emits a **typed plan**, and a **deterministic executor** applies it (no ReAct loop). Validated against the literature (typed-plan Ōē½ ReAct; control-flow-integrity against prompt injection). Built as **reusable SDK kits**, not ad-hoc ŌĆö see [[project_agentic_sdk_lives_in_loreweave_mcp_kit]]: this project will run many agents, so the pattern lives in `sdks/`.
>
> **Specs/plan:** architecture [2026-06-25-glossary-assistant-planner.md](../specs/2026-06-25-glossary-assistant-planner.md) ┬Ę kit mechanism [2026-06-25-plan-action-kit.md](../specs/2026-06-25-plan-action-kit.md) (Part I envelope/executor/error-classes, Part II concrete Go types + glossary op-set + caps + resolved-questions ledger) ┬Ę multi-agent execution plan [2026-06-25-plan-action-kit-phase1.md](../plans/2026-06-25-plan-action-kit-phase1.md).
>
> **The two new SDK kits (`sdks/go/`):**
> - **`loreweave_mcp`** gained the **plan-action kit** ŌĆö `plan.go` (frozen K0 contract: `Plan`/`Op`/`OpOutcome`/`Summary`, sentinel errors, `OpSpec`/`Registry`, `NewRegistry` panics on non-idempotent ops, `ValidatePlan` does dedupe + duplicate-conflict + frozen op-N ids + `MaxPlanOps=50` cap + reject-empty) and `execute.go` (`Execute(ctx,userID,plan,enabledOps,reg) Summary` ŌĆö tier-stable-sort, per-op `errors.Is`ŌåÆoutcome mapping, destructive-skip via `enabledOps`, internal-error abort). Constants: `PlanVersion=1`, `DescriptorExecutePlan="execute_plan"`, `PlanTokenTTL=30m`.
> - **`loreweave_llm` ŌĆö NEW Go LLM SDK (the missing piece; user-chosen this session).** Sync streaming client over the provider-registry LLM gateway. `client.go` (`Complete()` accumulates SSE token deltas; reasoning kept separate; internal `/internal/llm/stream?user_id=`+X-Internal-Token OR jwt `/v1/llm/stream`+Bearer), `models.go` (`StreamRequest`, `ReasoningEffort` incl. `none` to disable hidden thinking, `Result{Text,Reasoning,Usage,FinishReason}`), `errors.go` (code-matched sentinels), `sse.go` (8MB-buffer scanner). Zero deps.
>
> **Glossary wiring:** `planRegistry()` ([plan_ops.go](../../services/glossary-service/internal/api/plan_ops.go)) registers **4 additive ops** ŌĆö adopt_genres(t0), create_kinds(t1), add_attributes(t2), edit_attribute(t4); `glossary_plan` MCP tool ([action_plan_tools.go](../../services/glossary-service/internal/api/action_plan_tools.go)) resolves modelŌåÆreads ontologyŌåÆruns planner (120s + 1 repair round, loose-emitŌåÆvalidateŌåÆrepair)ŌåÆmints a confirm card; confirm/execute path ([plan_confirm.go](../../services/glossary-service/internal/api/plan_confirm.go) `effectExecutePlan` re-validates then `mcp.Execute`). chat-service `glossary_skill.py` routes multi-step goals to `glossary_plan` (K=2 stop).

> **Ō£ģ PHASE 2 SLICE 1 SHIPPED (this session) ŌĆö destructive ontology deletes + per-op enable toggle.** Plan [2026-06-25-plan-action-phase2-destructive.md](../plans/2026-06-25-plan-action-phase2-destructive.md). The planner can now emit **`delete_kind` / `delete_genre`** (tier 5, Destructive), and the confirm card renders each destructive op as an **opt-in checkbox (default OFF)** ŌĆö a bare "approve" never deletes (G1).
> - **Ops** ([plan_ops.go](../../services/glossary-service/internal/api/plan_ops.go)): 3 delete OpSpecs reuse the EXISTING soft-deprecate cascade primitives (`cascadeDeleteBookKind/Genre`, `softDeleteBookAttribute`); **deprecation-aware resolvers** split idempotency ŌĆö never-existedŌåÆ`target_gone` (failed), already-deprecatedŌåÆ`already_done` (skipped), liveŌåÆdelete (applied).
> - **The wire** ([action_confirm.go](../../services/glossary-service/internal/api/action_confirm.go) `decodeConfirmToken` now reads `enabled_ops`; [plan_confirm.go](../../services/glossary-service/internal/api/plan_confirm.go) `effectExecutePlan` validates ids (unknownŌåÆ422 `bad_enabled_op`) and passes the set to `mcp.Execute` ŌĆö **replacing the hardcoded `nil`** that made destructive ops impossible). Preview stamps `op_id`+`destructive` per row + cascade-count notes.
> - **FE** ([ConfirmActionCard.tsx](../../frontend/src/features/chat/components/ConfirmActionCard.tsx)): opt-in checkbox per destructive row + "N will be SKIPPED unless enabled" hint; [actionsApi.ts](../../frontend/src/features/chat/actionsApi.ts) sends `enabled_ops` only when Ōēź1 checked; i18n ├Ś4.
> - **­¤öÄ /review-impl ŌĆö 1 MED + 1 LOW-coverage FIXED, 1 regression caught:** **MED** ŌĆö `ontologyStateSummary` (the planner's ENTIRE view of current state) listed only kind codes ŌåÆ `delete_genre`/`delete_attribute` referenced codes the planner had never seen (always `target_gone`). Fixed: summary now lists **genres too** (cheap) so `delete_genre` works; **`delete_attribute` dropped from planner vocab** (kept registered+tested ŌĆö the `edit_attribute` precedent ŌĆö since per-attribute context would bloat every plan call AND the GUI already deletes attributes). **LOW** ŌĆö added a real-PG `delete_genre` round-trip (distinct cascade from kind). **Regression** ŌĆö the wider test net caught `TestPlanRegistry_BuildsWithoutPanic` asserting Phase-1's `len==4`/no-destructive invariant; updated to the Phase-2 shape (7 ops, exactly the 3 deletes destructive).

> **Ō£ģ PHASE 2 SLICE 2 SHIPPED (this session) ŌĆö planner orchestrates the merge action over detected duplicates.** Design+plan [2026-06-25-plan-action-phase2-merge-orchestration.md](../plans/2026-06-25-plan-action-phase2-merge-orchestration.md). **Why this and not "entity ops":** two scouts established that glossary **entities are UUID-identified and their names are non-unique EAV values** ŌåÆ a planner can't safely address an existing entity by name, AND merge/research already exist as class-C confirm actions (`descMerge`/`descDeepResearch`). The user chose the **"planner orchestrates existing actions"** path: the planner *selects* from detected `merge_candidates` (which already carry UUIDs) rather than inventing references.
> - **The op** ([plan_ops.go](../../services/glossary-service/internal/api/plan_ops.go) `merge_candidate`, tier 5 Destructive): params `{candidate_id, winner_id?}` ŌĆö the planner copies a **stable `candidate_id`** (one PK per cluster) from context, never a transcribed member-UUID or ambiguous name. Handler resolves the candidate's CURRENT members at execute time (no staleness), picks winner (`winner_id` if a member ŌåÆ else detector's `suggested_winner` ŌåÆ else `ErrBadParams`), and calls the EXISTING `mergeEntitiesCore` (journals + marks-merged). One op **per candidate** ŌåÆ each gets its own slice-1 enable toggle.
> - **Idempotency made independent of the best-effort `markCandidatesMerged` flip** (a test-driven fix ŌĆö it lags under load): statusŌēĀ`proposed`ŌåÆ`already_done` fast-path AND **0 losers actually merged ŌåÆ `already_done`**, so a re-run is deterministic. Bogus idŌåÆ`target_gone`.
> - **Planner context** ([action_plan_tools.go](../../services/glossary-service/internal/api/action_plan_tools.go) `mergeCandidatesSummary`, cap 25 by score, **best-effort** ŌĆö additive, never fails an ontology plan): each cluster's `candidate_id` + member names/ids/link-counts + suggested winner + rationale. Vocab + destructive guidance updated. Preview resolves candidateŌåÆwinner-kept/losers-merged names.
> - **­¤öÄ /review-impl ŌĆö 1 MED (prompt-injection) FIXED + tested:** the candidate context block embeds untrusted **entity names + detector rationale** (extraction/user content) into the planner's **SYSTEM** prompt ŌĆö the first free-text there (kind/genre codes are slugs). The user-supplied `reference` is fenced as DATA in the user message, but the candidate block was not. Fixed: `safePromptField` runs `sanitize.NeutralizeCanonText` (the canon-boundary defense the deep-research path already uses ŌĆö inert-izes chat-template/role markers + invisibles) + collapses whitespace (a smuggled newline can't forge a block field) + caps length. Regression tests assert a `<|im_start|>` / "ignore previous instructions" / newline-injected name is neutralized. (Blast radius was already bounded by the enable-toggle gate ŌĆö every destructive op is default-OFF + shown ŌĆö but untrusted text in the system prompt is a defense-in-depth gap worth closing.)
> - **VERIFY:** build/vet clean; kit green; **real-PG round-trip**: `merge_candidate` enableŌåÆ`applied` (loser soft-deleted via `mergeEntitiesCore`)ŌåÆre-runŌåÆ`already_done`ŌåÆbogusŌåÆ`target_gone`; +DB-free validate/parse/dedupe + `resolveMergeWinner` + neutralization unit; registry guard now **8 ops** (4 destructive). FE **unchanged** ŌĆö the slice-1 toggle already renders any destructive plan op (merge_candidate rows carry `op_id`+`destructive`). **ŌÅ│ chat-UI flow still `D-PLAN-EXEC-LIVE-SMOKE`** (now also covers the merge path).

> - ­¤¤Ī `D-EXTRACTION-BATCH-CONCURRENCY` ŌĆö **CORE SHIPPED (2026-06-26): the glossary-extraction worker now honors `concurrency_level`.** Root cause of "we can only run 1 LLM call, wastes the GPU": the translation-service extraction worker referenced `concurrency` **zero times** ŌĆö the per-(window├Śbatch) loop was a plain sequential `for`, and the knob never reached it. **Part A (propagate):** translation `create_extraction_job` `CreateExtractionJobPayload.concurrency_level` (Ōēż64) ŌåÆ published in the `extraction.job` message (no migration ŌĆö single publisher, no re-publish-from-row; redelivery carries it). **Part B (parallelize):** extracted the per-unit loop body into a nested `async def _run_unit` (no re-indent ŌĆö nested def keeps the 8-space body; `continue`ŌåÆ`return`), driven under an `asyncio.Semaphore(concurrency)` via `gather`; `concurrency<=1` keeps the EXACT sequential fast-path (back-compat). Chapters stay sequential (per-chapter known-entity accumulation); only a chapter's batches fan out. Safe because asyncio is single-threaded: the shared collectors (`_accept`/`all_entities`, `_record_outcome`/`batch_outcomes` keyed by stable `call_idx`, token sums via `nonlocal`, `batch_finish_reasons`) mutate atomically between awaits; cache keys are per-unit; writeback is whole-chapter post-loop. Hard ceiling `_EXTRACTION_MAX_CONCURRENCY=16`. VERIFY: py_compile clean; **18 extraction-worker tests** incl. new `test_batch_concurrency_runs_all_units_via_gather` (4 batches, concurrency=4 ŌåÆ 4 LLM calls, 4 outcomes, 4 entities, tokens summed) + 41 worker/truncation/cache/outcomes green (sequential path byte-unchanged). Ō£ģ **Trigger-side SHIPPED:** the `features/extraction` ExtractionWizard `StepConfirm` gained a **"Parallel LLM calls"** number input (1ŌĆō16, default 1) ŌåÆ `ExtractionJobRequest.concurrency_level` ŌåÆ `extractionApi.startJob` (sent only when >1). tsc clean; ExtractionWizard 3/3 green. So the full chain is live: FE field ŌåÆ payload ŌåÆ `extraction.job` message ŌåÆ worker semaphore. **Ō¢Č LIVE-SMOKE RAN (2026-06-26) ŌĆö REVEALED A DEEPER BOTTLENECK + a follow-up `D-PROVIDER-CONCURRENCY-CONFIG`.** Redeployed translation-service + worker (migration applied, worker has `_run_unit`+Semaphore). Drove a real extraction at concurrency=4 on the user's book (chapters 61-62) + the user's **Gemma 4 26B** (`019ebb6e`) via `mint_user_jwt` ŌåÆ `POST /v1/extraction/books/{id}/extract-glossary`. Job `019f009f` completed (9 batches all `ok`, 53 entities). **BUT the user reported "still only 1 call in LM Studio."** Root-caused: the worker correctly fans out 4 `submit_job` calls, but `submit_and_wait` is a **gateway job-submit+poll** path, and the gateway (provider-registry) **hardcaps LOCAL provider concurrency to 1** ŌĆö `ratelimit.maxFor`: `if localKinds[kind] { return 1 }` (`localKinds={ollama,lm_studio}`), sizing the queue's per-kind semaphore + the governor gate to 1 for local. So the 4 jobs serialize at the gateway ŌåÆ LM Studio sees 1. **This is an anti-pattern (user call): the platform must NOT bake a local/cloud topology assumption into code.** **DECISION (user): concurrency is a property of the specific PROVIDER CREDENTIAL** (the user's lm_studio endpoint / their OpenAI key+tier), set by them ŌĆö NOT per-kind, NOT per-cloud/local. **Ō¢Č `D-PROVIDER-CONCURRENCY-CONFIG` (next, M):** (a) **DB** ŌĆö `provider_credentials.max_concurrency INT` (default sensible); (b) **governor/queue refactor** ŌĆö key the semaphore by the **credential** (`concKey(credential_id)`, `semFor(credential_id)`) with the limit from the credential, replacing `localKinds`/`maxFor(kind)`; the queue's `KindResolver` ŌåÆ returns `(credential_key, limit)`; (c) **FE** ŌĆö a "Max concurrent requests" field in the BYOK provider/credential settings (ai-models). The worker-side concurrency (`c37629f1`) + wizard field (`73c7ff3e`) are correct + necessary ŌĆö they're just gated by this gateway cap until it's fixed. (The MCP `translation_start_extraction` agent path can also pass `concurrency_level` now.) (Note: knowledge-service KG-build extraction is a SEPARATE system with its own concurrency_level ŌĆö not this worker.)
> - Ō£ģ `D-PROVIDER-CONCURRENCY-CONFIG` ŌĆö **SHIPPED + LIVE-PROVEN (2026-06-26), provider-registry + FE.** The gateway no longer bakes a local/cloud topology assumption into code; concurrency is a property of the specific **provider credential** (the user's decision), and the **default is UNLIMITED** (request-as-demand ŌĆö neither the platform nor the user knows a cloud backend's real limit, so don't pretend to). **What changed:** (a) **migration** ŌĆö `provider_credentials.max_concurrency INT` NULL (NULL/Ōēż0 = unlimited). (b) **governor** ([governor.go](../../services/provider-registry-service/internal/ratelimit/governor.go)) ŌĆö deleted `localKinds`/`maxFor`/`MaxFor` (the `lm_studioŌåÆ1` hardcap); `Acquire(ctx, concClass, limit)` short-circuits to a no-op release when `limitŌēż0` (never touches Redis). `Guard`/`ConcurrencyGovernor` now take `(concClass, limit)`. (c) **queue** ([queue.go](../../services/provider-registry-service/internal/jobs/queue.go)) ŌĆö `KindResolver`ŌåÆ`ConcurrencyResolver` returns `(concClass, limit, ok)`; `semFor(class, limit)` returns **nil** (no gate) when unlimited, else a buffered chan of `limit`; `handleDelivery` skips the gate when nil; dropped `cloudMax` from `NewJobQueue`. (d) **resolver** ŌĆö new `repo.ResolveConcurrency` keys by `provider_credential_id` (user_model JOIN credential; platform_model ŌåÆ `platform:<id>`, unlimited); the worker computes `(concClass, concLimit)` once in `Process` (fail-open ŌåÆ unlimited) and threads it through `processChunks`/`streamWithRetry` (replacing `providerKind` in the Guard call); deleted the now-orphaned `ResolveKind`. (e) **CRUD contract** ŌĆö `max_concurrency` in create/list/get + a **present-aware PATCH** (`optionalInt` distinguishes absent=keep from null=clear-to-unlimited via `CASE WHEN $present THEN $val ELSE max_concurrency END`). (f) **FE** ŌĆö "Max concurrent requests" number field (''=unlimited) in `ProvidersTab` edit dialog (the AI-model provider editor) + `ProviderCredential.max_concurrency` type + create/patch payloads; i18n ├Ś4. **VERIFY:** go build + all go tests green (jobs/ratelimit/api; new `TestGovernor_UnlimitedPassesThroughWithoutRedis`, `TestSemFor_PerCredentialSizing`, `TestResolveConcurrency_*`); FE tsc 0 + settings vitest 19/19. **Ō£ģ LIVE A/B on deployed stack (Gemma/lm_studio, sampling concurrent `running` llm_jobs ŌĆö cache-independent):** cap=1 ŌåÆ **max 1 concurrent** (serialized); unlimited(default) ŌåÆ **max 4 concurrent** (the worker's `concurrency=4` fan-out now lands 4-wide on LM Studio ŌĆö the exact win the user wanted). **Ō£ģ LIVE PATCH round-trip:** set 4ŌåÆ`4`, patch unrelated fieldŌåÆ**keeps 4** (present-aware), clear nullŌåÆ**unlimited**. Credential left at unlimited. Closes the gateway-cap bottleneck that `D-EXTRACTION-BATCH-CONCURRENCY` surfaced; the worker fan-out (`c37629f1`) + wizard field (`73c7ff3e`) are now unblocked. **Known limitation (conscious):** the QUEUE `semFor` caches a credential's cap on first sight (a buffered chan can't resize), so ANY numericŌåÆnumeric runtime change (raise OR lower) needs a restart to affect the queue gate; switching TO unlimited is immediate (nil-sem path bypasses the cache). The Redis governor (per-CALL gate in `Guard`) reads the cap fresh each `Acquire`, so it honors a change immediately ŌĆö during a post-change/pre-restart window the effective cap is the TIGHTER of {cached queue cap, fresh governor cap} (safe: never exceeds the new value; a raise just lags). **Also:** cloud/platform models (NULL cap) are now UNLIMITED-by-default ŌĆö the old `GOVERNOR_CLOUD_MAX=8` per-kind cap is gone (the user's explicit "default unlimited"); backstop = the queue consumer pool (`workersŌēłGovernorCloudMax*2`, ~16), so one process still can't exceed ~16 concurrent jobs. **/review-impl fix folded in:** the queue's jobŌåÆclass resolver now FAILS-OPEN on a transient lookup error (runs ungoverned) instead of ack+dropping ŌĆö fail-closed stranded a `pending` job forever (the truth-sweeper only recovers stuck `running`); only a genuinely-gone model is dropped. Mirrors `Process`'s fail-open.
> - Ō£ģ `D-GLOSSARY-VC-FE-WIRING` ŌĆö **SHIPPED + BROWSER-SMOKED (2026-06-26), FE-only.** The user's "glossary version-control has a backend but was never wired to the FE" was *nearly* right: the entity-revision UI (`EntityHistoryPanel` + `useEntityRevisions` + types + i18n) was **fully built but stranded in dead code** ŌĆö `pages/book-tabs/EntityEditor.tsx`, a sidebar editor **never imported/mounted anywhere**. Users only ever see `components/entity-editor/EntityEditorModal.tsx` (rendered by `GlossaryTab.tsx`), which lacked it. Diff of the two editors: the dead one's ONLY delta was the History button ŌĆö the live modal supersedes it on every other axis (evidence tab, translations, i18n, typed cards). **Fix = migrate-then-delete:** (a) made `EntityHistoryPanel` embeddable (`embedded?: boolean` drops its overlay header + inner scroller so it renders as tab content; `onClose` now optional, default false keeps existing callers/tests unchanged); (b) added a **History tab** to `EntityEditorModal` reusing the panel + hook (lazy fetch on tab open, mirrors the evidences tab; restore ŌåÆ `load()` + `onSaved()` refresh); (c) `modal.tab.history` i18n ├Ś4; (d) **deleted the dead `EntityEditor.tsx`** (drift hazard). Backend (entity_revisions list/view/restore, async VG-1 projection capture, exact id-preserving reconcile) was already complete + wired. **VERIFY:** tsc 0; **105/105** glossary+entity-editor tests (new embedded-mode `EntityHistoryPanel` test); i18n parity 4 locales; no dangling ref to the deleted file. **Ō£ģ BROWSER-SMOKED on rebuilt frontend image** (test acct, Dracula book `019eeb09`, entity "The Night Journey" `019eeb22` w/ 4 revisions): History tab renders the revision list (localized ops/actors) ŌåÆ **View** expands the full whole-entity snapshot ŌåÆ **Restore** behind confirm ŌåÆ real server-side reconcile (entity reverted to rev #1: name viŌåÆen, translations 8ŌåÆ0 pruned) ŌåÆ captured as new reversible revision #5 (DB-confirmed) ŌåÆ surfaced in the list on refetch; restored back to #4 (translations re-added ŌåÆ 8) to leave data as found. Lesson saved: [[feedback_feature_wired_into_dead_editor_is_invisible]]. (Wiki revisions ŌĆö a SEPARATE VC system in the same service ŌĆö remain their own FE concern; not in scope.)

>   - `D-KG-TIMELINE-I18N` ŌĆö **Timeline tab mixes source-language + English.** Root cause: `/v1/knowledge/timeline` ([public/timeline.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/routers/public/timeline.py)) serves `:Event` nodes whose `title`/`summary`/`participants` are stored SOURCE-language by design, with **no `language` param + no Layer-2 localization** ŌĆö and `chapter_title_enricher.py` joins a book-/translated-language chapter heading onto each row (the visible mix). The multilingual design's C7 localization (plan [2026-06-23-kg-multilingual.md](../plans/2026-06-23-kg-multilingual.md)) only covered the **edge-timeline** in `graph_views.py`, never the Event timeline; spec still DRAFT. **Fix:** extend C7 to this endpoint (add `language`, resolve participant names via glossary join, pass `language` to chapter-title fetch). **DESIGN CALL:** free-text event `summary`/`time_cue` have no translation layer ŌĆö keep source-lang + "untranslated" marker (spec AC1) vs add a derived-translation layer. Size M. **Ō£ģ SHIPPED (2026-06-26), spec [docs/specs/2026-06-26-kg-timeline-localization.md](../specs/2026-06-26-kg-timeline-localization.md), user decision = BOTH (localize names/titles + translate summaries on-demand+cached like glossary):** **M1** ŌĆö `/v1/knowledge/timeline` resolves a reader language (explicit `?language=` ŌåÆ `get_reader_language` ŌåÆ none, mirroring graph_views) + threads it to the chapter-title fetch; **book-service** `/internal/chapters/titles` gained an optional `language` that LEFT-JOINs the sibling-language `chapters` row at the same `(book_id, sort_order)` (translated chapters are separate rows ŌĆö Q3 resolved) + `primaryLangSubtag` fold. **M2** ŌĆö `timeline_localizer.localize_participants` inverts participant NAMEŌåÆglossary_entity_id (`find_entities_by_name`, anchored-first) then reuses C7's `fetch_entity_display_names`; per-slot source-fallback + `*_translated` flag. **M3** ŌĆö new `event_text_translations` cache (glossary-shaped: PK(event_id,field,lang), confidence, source_hash staleness, project/user purge) + `EventTextTranslationsRepo` (coalesce-read, upsert-never-clobber-verified); `localize_event_text` fills from cache, misses fall back to source+marker (AC-T4) and fire a **pool-safe fire-and-forget** lazy translate via a NEW translation-service internal `POST /internal/translation/translate-text` (reuses `translate_text_core` ŌåÆ provider-registry, no SDK/no hardcoded model). FE: `TimelineEventRow` renders localized title/participants/summary with an accessible `SourceMarker` when a `*_translated` flag is false; `useTimeline`/`listTimeline`/`TimelineTab` forward `i18n.language`; i18n├Ś4. `Event` `@model_serializer` omits the 8 new fields when unset ŌåÆ AC-T5 byte-identical back-compat. **VERIFY:** knowledge-service unit suite **3113 pass** (11 new localization + 2 DDL); book-service build+tests (new `TestPrimaryLangSubtag`); translation py_compile; FE tsc 0 + TimelineTab 15/15; **integration smoke: knowledge redeployed HEALTHY + `event_text_translations` migration applied + clean startup**; book/translation healthy. **Deferred:** Ō£ģ `D-KG-TL-LIVE-SMOKE` ŌĆö **CLEARED (2026-06-26, rebuilt stack).** The **knowledgeŌåÆtranslation translate-and-cache** half is PROVEN live end-to-end: (a) direct `POST /internal/translation/translate-text` for the õĖćÕÅżńź×ÕĖØ owner (BYOK model `019ebb6eŌĆ”`) translated the real mixed-language event summary `"Zhang Ruochen is killed by his fianc├®e, Chi Yao."` ŌåÆ `"Zhang Ruochen bß╗ŗ vß╗ŗ h├┤n th├¬ cß╗¦a m├¼nh, Chi Yao, giß║┐t chß║┐t."` (knowledgeŌåÆtranslationŌåÆprovider-registry, no SDK/no hardcoded model); (b) the **full timeline cache cycle** on the test-account Dracula project `019ef35e` via the real `GET /v1/knowledge/timeline?language=vi`: read #1 = clean cache MISS ŌåÆ every field source-text + `*_translated=False` + lazy fill fires (AC-T4, GET never blocked); background fill wrote 3 events ├Ś {summary,title} into `event_text_translations`; read #2 = coalesce-read cache HIT ŌåÆ `summary_localized`/`title_localized` in Vietnamese + `*_translated=True`. Participant path also ran (English names ŌåÆ glossary anchor MISS ŌåÆ source + `translated=False`, the correct AC-T3 no-silent-mix outcome). **The knowledgeŌåÆbook vi-heading half could NOT be exercised** ŌĆö every live `:Event` on both candidate projects has `chapter_id=NULL` (events extracted without chapter linkage), so the M1 sibling-language heading join has no live row to hit; it stays unit-tested (`TestPrimaryLangSubtag` + book-service titles tests) until an extraction that links chapters lands. `D-KG-TL-PARTICIPANT-ANCHOR` (Option A: store `participant_entity_ids` on the Event node + backfill ŌĆö durable follow-up to M2's read-time name resolution).

API **is already paginated** (20/page, sort+filters) ŌĆö it does NOT load 10k at once. **One real COLLECTION bug:** writeback stamps `chapter_id` from `ChapterLinks[0]` (entity's FIRST chapter), not the quote's actual chapter ([extraction_handler.go:857](../../services/glossary-service/internal/api/extraction_handler.go#L857)). **Fixes:** (a) surface char offsets + `provenance_status` through API+FE w/ trust badge; (b) fix chapter pointer (pass per-quote chapter in payload); (c) "open source" link ŌåÆ reader@chapter+offset (refs overload `block_or_line` w/ URL ŌĆö render as link); (d) real pager (jump-to-page/page-size, keyset for deep offset). >   - `D-EXTRACT-ATTR-MERGE-DEFAULTS` ŌĆö **re-extraction appends evidence only, never updates attributes.** Root cause: every layer defaults the per-attribute merge action to **`fill`** = write-once-if-empty ŌåÆ already-filled attrs skipped (`fill_occupied`, [extraction_handler.go:1376](../../services/glossary-service/internal/api/extraction_handler.go#L1376)); FE/worker/DB (`merge_strategy DEFAULT 'fill_if_empty'`) all converge on fill. The engine **already supports `append`(multi-value)+`overwrite`** (tested) but nothing selects them + `append` isn't in the FE enum ([types.ts:29](../../frontend/src/features/extraction/types.ts#L29)). NOT a description/ontology-text issue. **Fix = config/code:** seed per-attribute `merge_strategy` (`append` for aliases/titles/abilities, `overwrite` for location/status, `fill` for identity) + expose `append` in FE; keep INV-8 verified-clobber guard. Reuse the raw cache to re-merge without re-LLM. `field_type=tags`ŌåÆ`append`; elseŌåÆ`overwrite`), CONDITIONAL on the untouched `fill_if_empty` default so author/admin overrides are never clobbered (idempotent). **M2** ŌĆö the resolver ([extraction_handler.go:1341](../../services/glossary-service/internal/api/extraction_handler.go#L1341)) now treats an explicit `"default"` action like an omitted one (defer to authored strategy); the worker default-profile builders ([extraction.py:159](../../services/translation-service/app/routers/extraction.py#L159), [mcp/server.py:729](../../services/translation-service/app/mcp/server.py#L729)) emit `"default"` instead of forcing `"fill"`; FE `AttributeAction` gains `default`+`append`, `StepProfile` auto-selects `default` (required stays `fill`) + adds Append/Default options & bulk buttons; i18n ├Ś4. **VERIFY:** real-PG `TestMergeStrategy_DefaultSentinel` (defaultŌåÆappend accumulates, defaultŌåÆoverwrite replaces) + full extraction-writeback suite green; FE tsc 0 + 8/8; **migration applied LIVE** on real glossary DB (distribution tagsŌåÆappend 3422, textareaŌåÆoverwrite 11673, text identity/state split, 1 pre-existing author `append` preserved); workerŌåÆglossary `extract-entities` **HTTP 200** on redeployed code (in-flight 4233-ch job 019f00e2 non-regressed). **Ō¢Č M3 SHIPPED (2026-06-26):** raw-cache re-merge HEAL to unfreeze ALREADY-extracted entities at $0 LLM. `replay_chapter_from_cache(use_authored_strategy=True)` ([extraction_replay.py](../../services/translation-service/app/workers/extraction_replay.py)) sends `attribute_actions={}` so the glossary resolver defers every attr to its authored strategy (append/overwrite), and adds a `"heal"` discriminator to the whole-chapter writeback_key so a chapter already written under the old frozen `fill` isn't deduped against the faithful write (re-running heal stays idempotent via the distinct key + per-item append dedup). New `rerun_merge_book` + endpoint `POST /internal/translation/extraction-cache/rerun-merge` ([internal_dispatch.py](../../services/translation-service/app/routers/internal_dispatch.py)) heals a whole book (or a `chapter_ids` subset), grant-gated EDIT + confirm-gated dry-run, bounded response (status counts + capped problem sample). **VERIFY:** 2 DB-free unit tests (aggregation + 50-cap) green; **LIVE on the user's running book:** dry-run ŌåÆ `mode:heal, would_write:60` for 2 cached chapters; confirm on 1 early chapter ŌåÆ `status_counts:{replayed:1}` (re-merged via translationŌåÆglossary under authored strategy, $0 LLM, no race with the in-flight 4233-ch job). **Use for the big book once its extraction is idle** to heal all chapters frozen under the old default.
> - ­¤¤Ī `D-LLM-FAILURE-RATE` ŌĆö **NEW (2026-06-26), in progress. DIAGNOSIS CORRECTED via live data.** Initial theory (truncation/thinking burning the output budget) was **disproven on the running stack**: `extraction_raw_outputs` over the live run shows `reasoning_effort=none` + `effort_band=none` on **every** call, **0** outputs containing `<think>`, `finish_reason=stop` everywhere (**no** `length`-truncation), and **151/155 batches clean**. All 3 workers already disable thinking correctly (extraction via `reasoning_fields`, chapter-translate hardcodes `enable_thinking:False` [session_translator.py:925](../../services/translation-service/app/workers/session_translator.py#L925), glossary-translate via `thinking_llm_fields`), and the gateway forwards `chat_template_kwargs` to lm_studio (only strips it for the *default* OpenAI cloud endpoint). So the `completed_with_errors` wall is **overstated** ŌĆö the flag fires on a SINGLE non-clean batch; real rate Ōēł5%, cause = **validation rejection on cleanly-finished output** (model occasionally returns wrong-kind / rarely-malformed JSON), NOT thinking/truncation. **Fix = structured-output enforcement (#1).** Ō£ģ **Gateway enabling slice SHIPPED:** `streamRequest.ResponseFormat` + `buildChatStreamInput` thread `response_format` into the adapter input (the `forwardOptionalChatFields` allowlist already lists it but nothing wired it from the request); absent-when-unset; build/vet clean + 3 `buildChatStreamInput` tests (forwards + omits-when-unset). Ō£ģ **Extraction worker slice SHIPPED:** `_entity_response_format(batch_kinds)` builds a LOOSE json_schema (top-level array; item requires `kind`=enum-of-batch-kinds + `name`; `additionalProperties:true` so per-kind profile fields stay free) attached as `response_format` when `_STRUCTURED_OUTPUT_ENABLED` (env `EXTRACTION_STRUCTURED_OUTPUT`, default ON); **safe fallback** ŌĆö the call is wrapped so a provider `LLMInvalidRequest` (model/server rejects the shape) **retries ONCE without response_format**, then a 2nd failure propagates as the real permanent error. py_compile clean; 2 new schema-builder tests + 25 existing extraction tests green. **Ō¢Č NEXT:** (a) same pattern for **glossary-translate** ([glossary_translate_worker.py](../../services/translation-service/app/workers/glossary_translate_worker.py)) + **chapter-translate** ([session_translator.py](../../services/translation-service/app/workers/session_translator.py)) ŌĆö their output schemas differ; (b) Ō£ģ **`D-LLM-STRUCTURED-OUTPUT-LIVE-SMOKE` ŌĆö PASSED (2026-06-26, deployed stack).** Verified the deployed provider-registry Go binary carries the `response_format` wiring + translation-worker has `_entity_response_format`. Then a real gateway call (`/internal/llm/stream`, test-account local **Qwen2.5 7B Instruct** `019eb620ŌĆ”`, the loose entity json_schema) returned **HTTP 200 + a clean conforming array** `[{"kind":"location","name":"royal martial arena"},{"kind":"character","name":"Zhang Ruochen"},{"kind":"character","name":"Eighth Prince"}]` (n=3, valid kinds from the enum). The **control WITHOUT response_format reproduced the bug**: malformed `["kind","Zhang Ruochen"]` arrays ŌåÆ PARSE FAILED ŌĆö the exact `validation_rejected`/`completed_with_errors` failure mode. Proves: gateway forwards it, lm_studio honors the json_schema (no LLMInvalidRequest ŌåÆ fallback not even needed for this model), and the schema converts malformed output into clean valid-kind output. Residual: a full extraction-JOB end-to-end (workerŌåÆoutcomes) wasn't driven (needs a cache-miss chapter), but the worker sends this exact response_format + is unit-tested. Ō£ģ **Glossary-translate slice SHIPPED + object-schema live-verified:** `attr_response_format(expected_codes)` (a JSON **object** of string-valued props keyed by the attribute codes, `additionalProperties:false`) in [glossary_translate_prompt.py](../../services/translation-service/app/workers/glossary_translate_prompt.py); the worker attaches it (gated by `TRANSLATION_STRUCTURED_OUTPUT`, default ON) + the same retry-without `LLMInvalidRequest` fallback. Kills the "Expecting ',' delimiter" parse failures that failed an entity. 3 schema tests + 102 translate-suite tests green; py_compile clean. **Object-schema live-smoke PASSED** (same gateway call, object json_schema ŌåÆ HTTP 200, valid `{"name":"ŌĆ”"}` object of strings ŌĆö lm_studio accepts the object shape too). Ōøö **Chapter-translate EXCLUDED from #1 ŌĆö NOT a JSON pipeline:** its output is `[BLOCK N]` marker TEXT ([block_batcher.py:254](../../services/translation-service/app/workers/block_batcher.py#L254) splits on the markers), so a JSON `response_format` would BREAK it; its failures are a different class (block-count/validation) already handled by its retry-with-correction loop. **#1 is COMPLETE for the two JSON pipelines (extraction + glossary-translate).** Ō£ģ **DEPLOY CONFIRMED (2026-06-26, rebuilt stack):** the running `infra-translation-worker-1`/`-service-1` image carries `attr_response_format` + both gate flags `EXTRACTION_STRUCTURED_OUTPUT` and `TRANSLATION_STRUCTURED_OUTPUT` **defaulting to `"1"`** (env unset ŌåÆ ON fleet-wide), so both JSON pipelines emit `response_format` live. Ō¢Č Remaining (optional only): a full extraction-job re-run on a cache-miss chapter to watch the `completed_with_errors` rate drop on real content (the gateway-level proof already showed the schema converts malformedŌåÆclean output; this is corroboration, not a gap). #3 (split-and-retry) deprioritized ŌĆö no current truncation. #2 (context_window) is fine ŌĆö user confirmed lm_studio loads 200K.
> - Ō£ģ `D-PLAN-MERGE-CONTEXT-COST` ŌĆö **CLEARED (2026-06-25).** `loadMergeCandidates` gained a `limit` arg: the planner passes `maxMergeCandidatesForPlan(=25)+1` so the SQL `LIMIT` caps BOTH the candidate rows AND the per-candidate member-detail load (`allMembers` only collects from the loaded set) ŌĆö every plan call now loads Ōēż26 clusters, not the whole `proposed` backlog. Highest-score clusters survive (ORDER BY score before LIMIT); `mergeCandidatesSummary(cands, more)` shows an honest "more candidates exist" note when the cap elided lower-score ones. FE inbox + pipeline-read tool pass `limit=0` (unlimited ŌĆö they want the full set to signal truncation), so their behaviour is unchanged. Kept the LIMIT over the alternative goal-keyword gate (multilingual goals make keyword gating fragile ŌĆö same call as delete-attr-vocab). VERIFY: build/vet clean; real-PG `TestLoadMergeCandidates_LimitCapsLoad` (limit=2 ŌåÆ top-2 by score; limit=0 ŌåÆ all 3) + merge/neutralization family green.
> - `D-PLAN-MERGE-DISMISSED-LABEL` ŌĆö **CONSCIOUS won't-fix (gate 5):** a `merge_candidate` op naming a `dismissed` candidate returns `already_done` (skipped) ŌĆö a slightly loose label (the merge never happened; it was rejected), but the OUTCOME (no merge, safe) is correct and the planner only ever sees `proposed` candidates in context. The kit's sentinel set has no "rejected" reason; not worth a new one.
> **Ō£ģ SLICE 2.5 SHIPPED ŌĆö `dismiss_candidate` (non-destructive sibling of merge).** Same candidate-id addressing; the planner emits `dismiss_candidate {candidate_id}` to REJECT a detected cluster as not-duplicates (flips statusŌåÆ`dismissed`, no entity touched), reusing the existing `dismissMergeCandidateCore`. **NON-destructive** ŌåÆ applies on a plain confirm with NO enable toggle (the real-PG test asserts exactly this via `f.confirm` without `enabled_ops`). Idempotency: statusŌēĀ`proposed`ŌåÆ`already_done`; bogusŌåÆ`target_gone`. Registry guard now **9 ops** (5 non-destructive incl. dismiss, 4 destructive). **­¤öæ also fixed a latent test-isolation bug:** the candidate real-PG tests were silently depending on another test creating `merge_candidates` first ŌĆö now they run the full idempotent `runMergeMigrations` chain (correct post-G4 `kind_id`ŌåÆ`book_kinds` FK), so they pass in isolation.
> - Ō£ģ `D-BATCH-RESEARCH-JOB` ŌĆö **FULLY SHIPPED + e2e-COMPLETE + review-passed 2026-06-25 (M1 + M2 + M3).** **/review-impl (3 fixed):** (1) FE progress tracked `items_processed` (visits, incl. free skips) ├Ę `items_total` (search cap) ŌåÆ could read ">100% / 13/10"; now `searches_run`├Ęcap, pinned 100% on complete. (2) launch-modal estimate `useEffect` re-ran on the unstable `fetchEstimate` closure ŌåÆ re-fetched + **clobbered the user's `max_entities`**; now a run-once ref guard. (3) resuming a `failed` job while another live job exists for the kind tripped the one-live unique index ŌåÆ 500; now mapped to a clean 409. **Ō£ģ FULL E2E on the deployed stack:** recreated `infra-glossary-service-1` with this branch ŌåÆ migration 0038 applied + ledgered + worker auto-started; deployed routes verified (list 200 / estimate 200 / create-bad-kind 404 / no-auth 401 via a minted test-account JWT); **full HTTP cycle**: `POST research-jobs` (owner-grant on an adopted owned book + 1 seeded entity) ŌåÆ `pending` ŌåÆ the **in-container worker** drained it ŌåÆ **`complete`** in 6s with `searches=1, sources=3` (real searxng "Mina Harker") ŌåÆ `GET status` confirmed live. Earlier: worker pipeline e2e (real searxng ŌåÆ evidence ŌåÆ complete on a throwaway DB) + stubbed worker unit tests. **Ō£ģ BROWSER E2E (Playwright, temp `infra-frontend` container on `infra_default` :5199 ŌĆö host vite shadows :5174):** logged in as the test account ŌåÆ book ŌåÆ Glossary ŌåÆ Ontology ŌåÆ Manage ŌåÆ selected the `character` kind ŌåÆ the **KindResearchPanel rendered** ("Web research" / "Research entities" / "No research job yet") ŌåÆ launch modal showed the **live estimate** ("1 of 1 entities ┬Ę ~$0.0100 indicative", max_entities defaulted to the entity count = fix #2 working) ŌåÆ typed a query ŌåÆ Start ŌåÆ the card polled pendingŌåÆrunningŌåÆ**Complete** ("Done ŌĆö 5 sources across 1 entities"); DB confirmed `complete searches=1 sources=5` + 5 reference-evidence rows. Also visually confirmed the case-2 genre/kind **delete trash is always-visible**. All artifacts cleaned up; temp container removed. **THE FEATURE IS FULLY e2e-PROVEN INCLUDING THE BROWSER UI.** **M3 (FE):** `researchApi.ts` (6 endpoints + estimate) ŌåÆ `useKindResearch` controller hook (react-query, polls at 2.5s while a job is active, stops on terminal) ŌåÆ `ResearchJobCard` (render-only per-state: pending/running+progress-bar/paused/complete/failed/cancelled with pause/resume/retry/cancel) + `ResearchLaunchModal` (query template with `{name}`, max_entities, max_results, pre-flight entity-count + indicative cost) + `KindResearchPanel` orchestrator, wired into `ManageWorkspace` under the selected kind. i18n ├Ś4 (`research.*`). VERIFY: `ResearchJobCard` render tests (per-state actions fire the right callbacks; complete is terminal-info; failed shows the error) + tiering suite 19 green; tsc clean; 4 locale JSONs valid. **M2 (FE-prior):** see below. Full design: [2026-06-25-glossary-batch-entity-research.md](../specs/2026-06-25-glossary-batch-entity-research.md).
> - ~~`D-BATCH-RESEARCH-JOB` M1+M2 line~~ ŌĆö superseded by the FULLY-SHIPPED line above. **M2 (worker + lifecycle):** in-process `RunResearchWorker` (started in main.go, gated on a web-search URL) drains `pending` jobs one entity at a time ŌĆö claim via `FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED` + stale-`running` reclaim (crash recovery), per-entity heartbeat/cursor persist (resume from `cursor_entity_id`), paid-search cap on `max_entities` (already-researched / anchorless entities skipped FREE via reference-evidence check), web-search error ŌåÆ `failed` without advancing cursor (resume retries). pause/resume/cancel endpoints + a shared `transitionResearchJob` (404 vs 409, clears error on resume). VERIFY: build/vet clean; real-PG worker tests (cap honoured + evidence attached + cursor; skip-already-researched is free; web-search-errorŌåÆfailed; pause/resume/cancel/404/409 state machine); **Ō£ģ live cross-service smoke** glossaryŌåÆprovider-registryŌåÆsearxng returned 3 real results (the worker's paid leg). Caught a real test bug: uuidv7's first 8 chars are a timestamp ŌåÆ non-unique name suffixes tripped `uq_entity_dedup`. **M3:** `ResearchJobCard` FE (states pending/running+progress/paused/complete/failed) + a "research all entities of this kind" launch on the kind + polling + i18n ├Ś4.
> - ~~`D-BATCH-RESEARCH-JOB` M1-only note~~ ŌĆö superseded by the M1+M2 line above. M1 = `entity_research_jobs` table (migration 0038, one-live-per-(book,kind) partial unique index) + `researchOneEntity` refactor (the per-entity web-search/neutralize/attach core now SHARED by the sync `glossary_deep_research` and the future worker ŌĆö deep-research tests green) + create/list/status endpoints + a pre-flight `research-estimate`. **BUILD REFINEMENT:** web-search returns no per-call cost (BYOK) ŌåÆ capped by **`max_entities`** (each = 1 search), `est_cost_usd` is indicative only, and the `paused_budget` state is dropped (the entity cap IS the scope). `researchJobMaxEntitiesHardCap=500`. VERIFY: build/vet clean; real-PG `TestResearchJob_CRUD_RealPG` (kind-validate, live count ignores soft-deleted, insertŌåÆload round-trip, list, one-live unique constraint, slot-frees-on-complete). **M2:** in-process worker draining `pending` jobs (reuse glossary's `internal/events/*` background pattern), skip-already-researched idempotency, `cursor_entity_id` crash-resume, pause/resume/cancel endpoints, cost-metering, live web-search smoke. **M3:** `ResearchJobCard` FE + launch action on the kind + polling + i18n ├Ś4. Full design (with superseding M1 note): [2026-06-25-glossary-batch-entity-research.md](../specs/2026-06-25-glossary-batch-entity-research.md).
> - ~~`D-BATCH-RESEARCH-JOB` (original design-checkpoint note)~~ ŌĆö **DESIGNED 2026-06-25 (gate 2: large/structural, XL cross-cutting).** The research-orchestration item was scoped: wiring it as a **synchronous plan op** is the wrong vehicle (the `execute_plan` executor runs in-request ŌåÆ a paid N-entity web-search fan-out would be minutes-long, timeout-prone, no progress/cancel; AND deep-research's "agent summarizes the attached evidence" design has no agent in the deterministic executor). **Decision (user, 2026-06-25): build it as an async batch-research JOB** hosted in **glossary-service** (it owns entities/evidence/the BYOK web-search; glossary already runs in-process `internal/events/*` consumers ŌĆö no need to proxy to knowledge like wiki does). Full design: [2026-06-25-glossary-batch-entity-research.md](../specs/2026-06-25-glossary-batch-entity-research.md) ŌĆö `entity_research_jobs` table + one-live-per-(book,kind) lock, a knowledge-modeled state machine (pendingŌåÆrunningŌåÆpaused_budget/paused_userŌåÆcomplete/failed/cancelled) with a per-search `max_spend` ceiling + cursor crash-resume, a `researchOneEntity` refactor that shares INV-6 web-search/neutralize/attach with the existing sync `glossary_deep_research`, 6 owner/grant-gated endpoints + pre-flight cost estimate, and a `ResearchJobCard` FE. Phased M1 (core+schema+endpoints) ŌåÆ M2 (worker+lifecycle) ŌåÆ M3 (FE). Entity create/edit/rename remain **out** (UUID-addressed, names ambiguous ŌĆö GUI/EAV only).

> # Ō¢ČŌ¢Č (prior epic) **Multilingual KG epic COMPLETE (M1ŌĆōM7)** ┬Ę branch `feat/kg-multilingual` ┬Ę 2026-06-24 ŌĆö ready to PR

> **EPIC: canonical-source + derived-localization multilingual KG.** Design fully clarified + red-teamed + gate-validated. Spec [2026-06-23-kg-multilingual.md](../specs/2026-06-23-kg-multilingual.md) ┬Ę detailed design [2026-06-23-kg-multilingual-detailed-design.md](../specs/2026-06-23-kg-multilingual-detailed-design.md) ┬Ę plan (7 milestones) [2026-06-23-kg-multilingual.md](../plans/2026-06-23-kg-multilingual.md). Design commit `1a448661`.
>
> **Core decisions (locked):** Layer-1 graph stays source-language, NEVER re-extracted from translations; derive a localization layer (L2 labels) + language-aware retrieval (L3) on top. vi content is **index-only + label-only**. **Gates:** V1 PASS (bge-m3 genuinely multilingual ŌåÆ single-vector-space premise holds), V7 PASS-caveat (reranker BYOK-optional ŌåÆ D5 must work WITHOUT it), V6 FAIL (lexical leg not CJK-tokenized ŌåÆ D12 real). D5 uses **soft language-preference weighting** (not hard filter ŌĆö robust to partial translation + dodges Neo4j post-filter limit).
>
> **Ō£ģ M1 FOUNDATION SHIPPED (this session) ŌĆö `source_lang` tag + cost metering + skip-gate.** All additive + dormant (source_lang unread until M4); no behavior change to existing retrieval.
> - **`source_lang`/`mixed`/`content_hash` on `:Passage`** (Neo4j-only ŌĆö DD1: passages have NO Postgres mirror) ŌĆö model + Cypher ON CREATE/MATCH + `upsert_passage` ([passages.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/db/neo4j_repos/passages.py)). New helpers `set_source_lang_for_source` (tag-only backfill) + `get_source_ingest_state` (skip-gate read: hash+canon+chapter_index).
> - **Ingester** ([passage_ingester.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/extraction/passage_ingester.py)): `resolve_source_lang` (declared chapter `original_language` wins, else `detect_primary_language`, "mixed"ŌåÆflag); **content-hash skip-gate** (skip embed+re-bill ONLY when text+canon+chapter_index all match ŌĆö a draftŌåÆpublish canon flip or reorder still re-ingests); **cost metering** via existing `record_spending` (DD2 ŌĆö fixes the pre-existing embed-token leak; `pool` threaded through the publish handler + backfill); `backfill_source_lang` (tag-only, per-chapter declared lang). `backfill_published_passages` now passes declared `source_lang` + `pool`; both wired at PUT embedding-model ([extraction.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/routers/public/extraction.py)).
> - **book-service** already returns `original_language` in `/internal/books/{id}/chapters` (server.go:2244) ŌåÆ no Go change; `BookClient.list_chapters` surfaces it verbatim.
> - **VERIFY:** knowledge-service unit suite **3017 passed** (source_lang/cost-metering/skip-gate tests + regression tests for canon-flip, chapter reorder, model-change, regional-subtag). **LIVE-SMOKE deferred ŌåÆ folds into M2's mandatory cross-service smoke** (M2 dual-indexes vi passages through this same ingest path + asserts source_lang) ŌĆö `D-KG-ML-M1-LIVE-SMOKE`.
> - **/review-impl (cold-start adversary) ŌĆö 1 HIGH + 2 MED FIXED, 2 LOW accepted:** **HIGH** ŌĆö the skip-gate ignored `embedding_model`; since the model-set path's `_delete_project_graph` deletes only graph labels (`Entity/Event/Fact/ExtractionSource`), NOT `:Passage`, a model change would have skip-gated re-embedding ŌåÆ stale-dimension vectors. Fixed: `get_source_ingest_state` returns `embedding_model` + the skip condition compares it (re-ingests on model change). **MED** ŌĆö draft-index path (`raw_search.py`) didn't meter embed spend ŌåÆ now passes `pool` (guarded); + `source_lang` now normalized to ISO-639-1 primary subtag (`zh-CN`ŌåÆ`zh`) so M4 ranking won't drift. **Accepted+documented (LOW):** `get_source_ingest_state` LIMIT-1 is safe (one ingest writes identical hash/canon/index/model to all chunks); `record_spending` best-effort silent-fail is intentional.
>
 **Ō£ģ M2 EVENT + DUAL-INDEX SHIPPED (this session).** A chapter's ACTIVE translation is now dual-indexed as `source_lang`-tagged `:Passage` nodes ŌĆö index-only (NEVER re-extracts Layer 1, R1).
> - **Producer (translation-service):** `translation.published` outbox event (`aggregate_type='translation'` ŌåÆ `loreweave:events:translation`) emitted at ALL 3 active-version chokepoints (DD5): manual `set_active_version`, human-block-edit `patch_translation_block`, and the auto-promote in `chapter_worker._finalize_chapter` (the one that emitted NOTHING before ŌĆö only when the promote actually changed the active row). One envelope via [translation_events.py](../../services/translation-service/app/translation_events.py).
> - **vi-text retrieval:** new internal `GET /internal/translation/chapters/{chapter_id}/active-text?target_language=` ([main.py](../../services/translation-service/app/main.py)) returns the active version's flat text (translated_body, else assembled from translated_body_json) ŌĆö the translated text lives in translation-service, NOT book-service.
> - **Consumer (knowledge-service):** new [translation_client.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/clients/translation_client.py) + `handle_translation_published` ([handlers.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/events/handlers.py)) resolves project by book_id ŌåÆ fetches vi text ŌåÆ `ingest_chapter_passages(text_override=vi, source_lang=vi, canon=True)`. Registered: stream `loreweave:events:translation` ([consumer.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/events/consumer.py)) + dispatcher ([main.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/main.py)).
> - **Language-scoped passage identity (DD1):** `passage_canonical_id` now includes `source_lang` so vi/zh chunks of the SAME chapter (shared `source_id=chapter_id`, kept clean for hitŌåÆchapter mapping) are DISTINCT nodes; `delete_passages_for_source` + `get_source_ingest_state` gained a `source_lang` filter so re-ingesting vi never wipes zh (and vice-versa). vi uses `source_type='chapter'` ŌåÆ the existing retriever finds it (M4 adds language ranking). Empty/'unknown' lang keeps pre-M2 ids byte-identical.
> - **VERIFY:** knowledge unit **3022 passed** (canonical-id distinctness, text_override bypass, handler dual-index/skip-no-text/skip-no-project); translation-service **137 relevant + 2 emit-envelope** tests pass, py_compile clean.
> - **Ō£ģ LIVE-SMOKE PASSED (2026-06-24, on the running stack) ŌĆö clears `D-KG-ML-M2-LIVE-SMOKE` + `D-KG-ML-M1-LIVE-SMOKE`.** Target: Dracula book `019ef35c-36c3-ŌĆ”` / chapter `019ef35c-3f4c-ŌĆ”` (active vi translation, knowledge project `019ef35e-d08f-ŌĆ”`, embed model `019e7f71-ŌĆ”`). **(1) Endpoint:** `active-text?target_language=vi` ŌåÆ 5939 chars assembled from `translated_body_json`. **(2) Event path:** inserted `translation.published` outbox row in `loreweave_translation` ŌåÆ worker-infra relay (`published_at` set) ŌåÆ `loreweave:events:translation` ŌåÆ `knowledge-extractor` group ŌåÆ handler ŌåÆ **6 vi `:Passage`** created (was 0; the 6 originals are `source_lang=null`). **(3) Data model (DD1):** vi passages carry `source_lang='vi'`, clean `source_id=chapter_id`, `embedding_1024` (dim=1024, V1), `canon=TRUE`, `content_hash` set. **(4) Idempotency:** re-emit (relayed + consumed, lag 0) ŌåÆ still **6** vi passages, no dupes/re-embed. **ŌÜĀ The smoke caught a real defect mock-VERIFY can't:** the deployed knowledge-service image was **stale** (cached Docker `COPY` layer) ŌåÆ running consumer subscribed to only 3 streams (no `translation`) ŌåÆ the event would've been silently dropped. Fixed via `--no-cache` rebuild + `--force-recreate`; confirmed the consumer now subscribes to all 4 streams. (Cost-metering `record_spending` is best-effort/non-blocking ŌĆö lightly covered live; phantom-pricing tracked separately in `D-KG-ML-EMBED-PRICING`.)
>
> **Ō£ģ M3 READER-LANGUAGE STORAGE SHIPPED (this session) ŌĆö book-service only.** Per-(user,book) reader-language preference, server SSOT + cross-device (DD3).
> - **Table** `user_book_prefs(user_id, book_id, reader_language, updated_at, PK(user_id,book_id))` in book-service schema ([migrate.go](../../services/book-service/internal/migrate/migrate.go)) ŌĆö no FK (matches `reading_progress`; existence gated at handler).
> - **Public endpoints** `GET/PUT /v1/books/{book_id}/reader-language` ([reader_language.go](../../services/book-service/internal/api/reader_language.go)) ŌĆö view-gated via `canViewOrPublic` (grant OR public book, so a reader of a shared/public novel keeps a pref; non-grantee private book ŌåÆ 404, no oracle). PUT validates a BCP-47-ish tag (`langTagRe`, Ōēż35 chars); empty/whitespace ŌåÆ clears (delete ŌåÆ null). NOT localStorage (CLAUDE.md). Registered NOT in the mutating-grant table (per-user data = view-tier write, like favorites); GET added to `grant_mapping_test` readRoutes.
> - **Internal resolver** `GET /internal/books/{book_id}/reader-language?user_id=` (token-gated, user_id param like `getBookAccess`) ŌĆö the cross-service source for M4 retrieval + M7 consumers. The FALLBACK chain (reader-pref ŌåÆ detected query lang ŌåÆ source lang) lives at the CONSUMER (M4) where query+source langs are known ŌĆö M3 only exposes the stored value.
> - **VERIFY:** book-service `go build`+`go vet` clean; `internal/api` green (ReaderLang nil-pool auth/tenancy/validation + grant-mapping). **Ō£ģ LIVE-SMOKE on real PG18** (`loreweave_book_m3smoke` throwaway): setŌåÆGETŌåÆ**AC3 cross-request read**ŌåÆinternal-resolverŌåÆupsertŌåÆclearŌåÆnull + **per-user isolation** (two users, independent prefs, no shared row) all PASS. Full book-service suite green ŌĆö also fixed a **pre-existing** stale `config.TestLoadValidation` (didn't set the now-required `CONFIRM_TOKEN_SIGNING_SECRET`; one-line, same-service, fix-now).
>
> **Ō£ģ M4 LANGUAGE-AWARE RETRIEVAL SHIPPED (this session) ŌĆö D5 soft-weighting (cross-service: book + knowledge).** The core user-facing win: a reader's language preference softly re-ranks hybrid search so they get content in their language first.
> - **Both legs carry `sourceLang`:** semantic from the passage prop ([retriever.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/search/retriever.py) `passage_to_hit`); lexical from the chapter's `original_language` ŌĆö book-service `lexical-search` now selects `c.original_language` in all 3 SQLs + threads it through `buildLexicalHit` ([search.go](../../services/book-service/internal/api/search.go)).
> - **Soft language ordering, [hybrid_fusion.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/search/hybrid_fusion.py) `apply_language_preference`** (revised by /review-impl ŌĆö see below): a **stable matched-first partition** applied as the FINAL ordering pass (post-rerank, post-floor, pre-cap). Reader-language hits move to the front, preserving the upstream rerank/RRF order within the matched + unmatched groups; nothing is dropped (soft, robust to partial translation). `"mixed"` matches any pref. `normalize_lang` = ISO-639-1 primary subtag (mirrors M1 `resolve_source_lang`). Scale-independent (no weight to tune).
> - **­¤öÄ /review-impl ŌĆö 2 HIGH FIXED (both masked by the original smoke):** (1) the boost was **inert under rerank** ŌĆö applied pre-rerank, but `pool_n Ōēź len(fused)` always, so the cross-encoder re-sorts the whole list by relevance and discards it ŌåÆ moved the language step to the FINAL pass + made it scale-independent (the additive `w_lang`/`settings.lang_pref_weight` is removed). (2) the RRF fusion key `_hit_key` **omitted `sourceLang`**, so a chapter's vi + zh/en passages (same `chapterId`+`surface`+`chunkIndex`) collided and one language was dropped BEFORE ranking ŌåÆ added `sourceLang` to `_hit_key` (mirrors `passage_canonical_id`/DD1). Both have regression tests (rerank-on ordering; both-languages-survive-fusion). Re-smoked live (rerank default-on path): `language=en`ŌåÆ6 en then 6 vi (**n=12, both survive**); `language=vi` & reader-prefŌåÆ6 vi then 6 en.
> - **Resolver chain** ([raw_search.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/routers/public/raw_search.py)): `?language=` ŌåÆ the CALLER's stored reader-language (M3, via new `BookClient.get_reader_language` ŌåÆ `/internal/books/{id}/reader-language`) ŌåÆ `detect_primary_language(query)` ŌåÆ None. It's the SEARCHER's pref (caller), not the resolve-to-owner project user. `run_hybrid_search` gained `pref_lang` (None default ŌćÆ wiki in-process path unchanged).
> - **VERIFY:** book-service `go build`+`go vet` clean, api tests green; knowledge **98 unit** pass (boost math/sort/mixed/regional, resolution chain, both legs carry sourceLang); provider-gate OK. **Ō£ģ LIVE-SMOKE (cross-service) on the Dracula stack:** reader-pref `vi` resolved via book-service internal endpoint ŌåÆ boost applied (`score=fused+0.0164`); with **identical-embedding en/vi clones** (language = sole variable) `language=en`ŌåÆen ranks #0, `language=vi` & reader-prefŌåÆvi ranks #0 ŌĆö the boost alone flips ranking, M3ŌåÆM4 chain end-to-end. (Confirmed the M2 dual-index is bug-free: a clean re-index yields exactly 6 vi; an earlier "12 passages" was smoke-data pollution from consumer replay-from-0 + repeated manual emits, now reset.)
>
> **Ō£ģ M5 PART 1 SHIPPED (this session) ŌĆö C5 predicate labels + C4 kind name_i18n.** M5 is larger than the plan's "size M" (4 features across glossary + knowledge); sliced by risk boundary.
> - **C5 predicate labels (DD7, knowledge-service):** [predicate_labels.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/labels/predicate_labels.py) ŌĆö `humanize(snake)` fallback + curated vi map for the ~24 common KG predicates (open vocab ŌćÆ unknown predicates degrade to humanize). Endpoint `GET /v1/knowledge/predicate-labels?language=&codes=` ([labels.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/routers/public/labels.py)) ŌĆö backend-served (R5), `codes` resolves a set else returns the catalog. 13 tests.
> - **C4 kind name_i18n (DD4, glossary-service):** migration `0037_kind_name_i18n` ([kind_name_i18n.go](../../services/glossary-service/internal/migrate/kind_name_i18n.go)) ŌĆö `name_i18n JSONB` on `system_kinds`/`user_kinds`/`book_kinds` (inherits each tier's scope, no new tenancy surface) + admin-seeds the 12 System kinds in vi (en `name` stays the en label). The internal ontology reads (`/internal/users/{id}/glossary-standards`, `/internal/books/{id}/ontology`) now surface `name_i18n` via `loadKindNameI18n` (JSONBŌåÆ[]byteŌåÆunmarshal, graceful nil on un-migrated DB), tier-correct (a user kind shadows by code AND label). **Tenancy:** System is admin-seed-only (never a user write ŌĆö the `entity_kinds` bug); per-user/book label authoring deferred (`D-KG-ML-PERUSER-LABELS`). Verified on real PG: `character`ŌåÆ"Nh├ón vß║Łt", `location`ŌåÆ"─Éß╗ŗa ─æiß╗ām".
>
> **Ō£ģ M5 PART 2 SHIPPED (this session) ŌĆö C9 entity-name vi read + C7 graph-view/timeline label resolution + the SystemŌåÆbook label-inheritance fix.** AC1 now works: a vi reader's KG graph-view shows vi kind, entity-name + predicate labels.
> - **C9 (glossary-service):** new internal `POST /internal/books/{book_id}/entity-display-names` ([entity_display_names_handler.go](../../services/glossary-service/internal/api/entity_display_names_handler.go)) ŌåÆ `{entity_id, display_name, translated}[]`, resolving each entity's `name`/`term` attr to a language via the SAME `attribute_translations` join as the public `display_language` list (reuses the existing translate pipeline's idempotent population ŌĆö no new write/migration). Internal-token gated, book-scoped, soft-absent for unknown ids. The entity-name vi **population** rides the existing batch-translate (`internalApplyTranslations`, idempotent on `(attr_value_id, language_code)`, never overwrites `verified`); C9 is the READ the KG joins on.
> - **C7 (knowledge-service):** `GraphNode` gains `kind_label`/`name_label`, `GraphEdge` gains `edge_type_label`, `EdgeTimeline`/`TimelineInstance` gain `edge_type_label`/`target_label_localized` (all additive, None ŌćÆ canonical source-fallback = AC1's "explicit fallback marker"). Pure decorators [graph_labels.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/labels/graph_labels.py) + reader-lang param hygiene [reader_lang.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/labels/reader_lang.py) (shared with M4). `read_graph` + `read_edge_timeline` ([graph_views.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/routers/public/graph_views.py)) gained `?language=` ŌåÆ caller's reader-pref fallback; resolve kindŌåÆ`name_i18n[lang]` (glossary ontology client, now carrying `name_i18n` + a `kind_labels(lang)` helper), predicateŌåÆ`resolve_predicate_label` (C5), entity nameŌåÆglossary C9 batch (TRANSLATED-only, so name_label=None is an honest fallback). Best-effort throughout (glossary outage ŌåÆ canonical graph, never 500). `read_graph` reuses the grant gate's already-resolved `(owner, book_id)` (project_meta_dep) ŌĆö no extra query.
> - **­¤öæ Real gap caught by the live-smoke + fixed now (correctness):** for a BOOK-bound KG project, kind labels resolve from `get_book_ontology`ŌåÆ`book_kinds.name_i18n`, which is EMPTY (C4 seeded only System). Per the LOCKED tier-merge (System defaults ŌåÆ Per-book, higher shadows by code), a book kind must INHERIT the System vi label unless overridden. `internalBookOntology` (and the user-standards shadow path) now merge `system_kinds.name_i18n ŌŖĢ tier.name_i18n` via a new `mergeLabels` ([internal_ontology_read.go](../../services/glossary-service/internal/api/internal_ontology_read.go)). Without this, book-bound graph-views (the common case) showed NO kind labels.
> - **VERIFY:** knowledge unit **3067 passed** (new: 11 graph_labels pure + 1 graph-view localization router + 3 glossary-client `fetch_entity_display_names` + 5 existing graph-view tests fixed for the new deps); glossary `go build`/`vet` clean + real-PG tests green (C9 4 tests, book-ontology System-inheritance, GlossStandards). provider-gate OK. **Ō£ģ AC1 LIVE-SMOKE PASSED (cross-service: knowledgeŌåÆglossaryŌåÆPGŌåÆNeo4j):** seeded a vi name translation + a 2-node `character`/`ALLY_OF` graph on book `019ed9d0ŌĆ”`; `GET /v1/kg/projects/{id}/graph?language=vi` ŌåÆ node `kind_label="Nh├ón vß║Łt"` (System label inherited by the book kind ŌĆö proves the merge fix live), `name_label="A-ri-a"` (C9), edge `edge_type_label="─æß╗ōng minh cß╗¦a"` (C5); untranslated node ŌåÆ `name_label=null`; **no-language ŌåÆ fully canonical (back-compat)**. Smoke artifacts cleaned up.
>
> **Ō£ģ M6 SHIPPED (this session) ŌĆö CJK lexical leg via a Neo4j `cjk` full-text index (D12 / V6).** A short Chinese/Japanese/Korean keyword (the pg_trgm-FAIL case) now recalls the right passage.
> - **V6 probe (decisive):** `db.index.fulltext.listAvailableAnalyzers()` ŌåÆ Neo4j ships a built-in **`cjk`** analyzer (bi-grams); Postgres `pg_available_extensions` ŌåÆ only `pg_trgm` (NO `zhparser`/`pg_jieba` ŌåÆ those would need a custom image = infra change). **Decision (per the plan's "pick by probe + infra cost"): Neo4j `cjk`** ŌĆö zero infra change, and `:Passage` already carries `source_lang` (M1/M2). The book-service Postgres `language`-param/`pg_jieba` route from the plan is therefore moot; book-service trigram stays as the script-agnostic fallback (its `ILIKE` substring still finds exact CJK, just unranked/unindexed).
> - **Schema:** `CREATE FULLTEXT INDEX passage_text_cjk_ft ŌĆ” OPTIONS {indexConfig:{fulltext.analyzer:'cjk'}}` over `:Passage(text)` ([neo4j_schema.cypher](../../services/knowledge-service/app/db/neo4j_schema.cypher), idempotent, applied on startup).
> - **Repo:** `find_passages_by_fulltext` + `lucene_escape` + `PASSAGE_CJK_FT_INDEX` ([passages.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/db/neo4j_repos/passages.py)) ŌĆö global FT index ŌåÆ oversample + post-filter on `user_id`/`project_id`/`source_lang`/`canon` (mirrors `find_passages_by_vector`); raw query Lucene-escaped (CJK runs kept) so a stray `+`/`:` can't break the parse; blank ŌåÆ no call.
> - **Retriever:** new `query_has_cjk` (codepoint test ŌĆö a bare proper noun is too short for language detection) + a `_cjk_lexical` leg ([retriever.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/search/retriever.py)) fused as a THIRD RRF input, gated on `mode!=semantic AND query_has_cjk`. **Additive ŌĆö empty for Latin queries, so non-CJK retrieval is byte-identical to pre-M6.** Best-effort (FT index missing / query error ŌåÆ `degraded['cjk_lexical']`, never 500). Needs no embedding model (works on an un-embedded project).
> - **VERIFY:** knowledge unit **3076 passed** (new: `query_has_cjk`, `lucene_escape`, `find_passages_by_fulltext` wiring + blank-skip, retriever CJK-leg-fuses + Latin-skips; an autouse stub makes the pre-existing CJK-query retriever tests see the leg as additive-empty). **Ō£ģ AC LIVE-SMOKE PASSED:** seeded a zh passage (`µ¤│µŚĀÕ░śĶĄ░õĖŖÕż®ÕēæÕ│░’╝īĶ¦üÕł░õ║åÕ¦£ÕŁÉńēÖŃĆé`) under the real book owner; `search?query=Õż®Õēæ&mode=lexical` (**2-char** ŌĆö the exact pg_trgm-FAIL case) ŌåÆ returns the block (cjk leg, relevance 2.53); `query=Õ¦£ÕŁÉńēÖ` (3-char) ŌåÆ relevance 5.05; `degraded={}`. Smoke artifacts cleaned up. The cjk index was confirmed created on container startup.
> - **Note (cosmetic):** `passage_to_hit` labels every passage hit `matchType:"semantic"` regardless of which passage leg (vector vs cjk-FT) found it ŌĆö a passage-node-vs-chapter-node distinction, not a correctness issue. Tracked as `D-KG-ML-M6-MATCHTYPE` if it ever matters for UI.
>
> **Ō£ģ M7 SHIPPED (this session) ŌĆö consumer wiring + headline E2E. THE EPIC IS COMPLETE.** Reader-language now threads end-to-end so a vi reader gets vi-first grounding + an honest coverage note.
> - **knowledge (C12):** `drawers/search` + raw-search take `?language=` ŌåÆ soft matched-first partition (not a filter ŌĆö off-language hits still surface) + a `coverage` note ({reader_lang,total,in_language,partial,note}) via new `hybrid_fusion.language_coverage`; each drawer hit carries `source_lang` ([drawers.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/routers/public/drawers.py), [raw_search.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/routers/public/raw_search.py)).
> - **composition (C6):** `pack()` resolves the author's reader-language ONCE (`BookClient.get_reader_language` ŌåÆ book-service M3 internal) + threads `language` into the glossary `select_for_context` (per-language aliases) + knowledge `search_drawers` (vi-first lore) lenses, base + delta ([pack.py](../../services/composition-service/app/packer/pack.py), [lenses.py](../../services/composition-service/app/packer/lenses.py)). Best-effort (None ŌåÆ source-language).
> - **chat:** already wired (S6 `display_language` ŌåÆ `build_context`); the FE sends the reader language.
> - **FE (C12):** drawers search sends the UI language; `RawDrawersTab` shows the coverage banner; `DrawerResultCard` badges each hit's `source_lang`; +i18n (en/vi/ja/zh-TW).
> - **VERIFY:** knowledge unit **3079** + composition pack/client + FE **20** green; tsc clean; provider-gate OK. **Ō£ģ HEADLINE E2E LIVE (Ōēź3 services):** (1) drawers `?language=vi` on identical-embedding vi/zh clones ŌåÆ **vi first** (cosine tied 0.4888 ŌćÆ ordering is purely the language partition) + `coverage {total:2,in_language:1,partial:true}`; (2) book-service internal reader-language ŌåÆ `vi`; (3) **composition grounding** `works/{p}/scenes/{n}/grounding` (composition pack ŌåÆ book reader-lang=vi ŌåÆ knowledge drawers/glossary `language=vi` ŌåÆ provider-registry embed) ŌåÆ `grounding_available=true, token_count=4589, no warnings`. **Ō£ģ FE browser smoke (Playwright):** on the built bundle the drawers Raw tab renders the **coverage banner** + a per-hit **language badge** on every hit (`vi├Ś7, zh├Ś1`), driven by the live API. **GOTCHA:** a pre-existing **host vite-dev server** (a node process from a different/stale working copy) shadows :5174 ahead of the docker nginx container ŌĆö so the built FE was smoked via a temp container on the compose network (`docker run --network infra_default -p 5199`). Also fixed a pre-existing **infra drift**: recreating the frontend cascaded an `api-gateway-bff` restart that crash-looped on a missing `ROLEPLAY_SERVICE_URL` (the BFF `requireEnv`'s it; compose never set it) ŌåÆ added it to the BFF env in [docker-compose.yml](../../infra/docker-compose.yml).
>
> **­¤Äē EPIC DONE ŌĆö the driving scenario works:** a vi reader who can't read the zh/en source gets KG content, kind/predicate/entity labels, lexical recall, retrieval ranking, cowrite grounding, and coverage signalling in Vietnamese. **Branch `feat/kg-multilingual` is ready to PR into main.** Remaining tracked rows are all out-of-scope deferrals (see below). **Suggested next:** open the PR, or pick up `D-KG-ML-M4-EVAL` (formal multilingual eval golden-set) when a corpus exists.
>
> **Ō¢Č Deferred ŌĆö debt-minimisation pass (2026-06-24): the list was triaged + shrunk; only 3 genuine rows remain, each clearing the defer-gate.**
> - `D-KG-ML-M4-EVAL` ŌĆö **BLOCKED (gate 4):** formal multilingual eval golden-set needs a curated corpus + judge ensemble (large/structural). The matched-first partition is parameter-free (no weight to sweep); the mechanism is proven by unit tests + the live reordering smoke. Target: when a multilingual eval corpus exists.
> - `D-KG-ML-WIKI` ŌĆö **OUT OF SCOPE (gate 1):** per-language wiki article BODIES is a separate feature (translate long-form wiki text), not part of the KG localization layer.
> - `D-KG-ML-PERUSER-LABELS` ŌĆö **OUT OF SCOPE (gate 1/5):** per-user kind-label AUTHORING UI/API. System+Book tiers cover the scenario today (a user overrides a label via a book kind; C4 merge gives System defaults). Build only if users ask to author their own per-user kind labels.
> - `D-KG-ML-DRAWERS-METER` ŌĆö **PRE-EXISTING (not this branch) + product decision (gate 4/5):** whether read-path search embeds should bill the user's BYOK budget. `drawers/search` computes `embedding_cost_usd` but doesn't `record_spending`; this predates kg-multilingual. Deferred pending a product call.
>
> **Ō£ģ Recently cleared (this debt pass):**
> - Ō£ģ `D-KG-ML-M6-MATCHTYPE` ŌĆö **FIXED:** `passage_to_hit` gained a `match_type` param; the cjk full-text leg now labels its hits `"lexical"` (was the hardcoded `"semantic"`). +retriever test.
> - Ō£ģ `D-KG-ML-MULTI-TRANSLATION-LANG` ŌĆö **CLEARED:** design proven ŌĆö `test_canonical_id_distinct_per_language` now asserts zh+vi+en translations of ONE chapter are 4 distinct ids (incl. back-compat). (A live smoke with a real 2nd target language is naturally blocked until a book has one; the coexistence design is proven.)
> - Ō£ģ `D-KG-ML-PERLANG-EMBED` ŌĆö **WON'T-FIX:** the V1 probe PASSED (bge-m3 is genuinely multilingual ŌåÆ single-vector-space premise holds), so a per-language embedding model is unnecessary. Reopen only if a future target language shows weak recall.
> - Ō£ģ `D-KG-ML-EMBED-PRICING` ŌĆö **VERIFIED MITIGATED:** the phantom-cost concern was "`cost_per_token` gets a model UUID ŌåÆ fallback rate", but BOTH the ingest ([passage_ingester.py:432](../../services/knowledge-service/app/extraction/passage_ingester.py)) and drawers ([drawers.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/routers/public/drawers.py)) pass `embed_result.model` (the RESOLVED provider name); local models map to $0 via the `bge-`/`ollama/`/`lm_studio/` prefixes in [pricing.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/pricing.py). Residual edge (a provider reporting a non-name model string) folds into pricing.py's existing "swap for a dynamic pricing table" note ŌĆö not this branch's debt.
> - Ō£ģ `D-KG-ML-M2-LIVE-SMOKE` + Ō£ģ `D-KG-ML-M1-LIVE-SMOKE` (live-smoke PASSED 2026-06-24).

# Ō¢Č PRIOR ŌĆö **interview-roleplay POC COMPLETE (M1ŌåÆM7)** ┬Ę branch `feat/interview-roleplay` ┬Ę HEAD `112c907d` ┬Ę 2026-06-24

> **Anti-drift interview-practice roleplay ŌĆö the POC for the future roleplay-service ŌĆö is DONE end-to-end (M1ŌĆōM7).** Spec [2026-06-23-interview-roleplay.md](../specs/2026-06-23-interview-roleplay.md), plan [2026-06-23-interview-roleplay.md](../plans/2026-06-23-interview-roleplay.md). Brain-analogy anti-drift: `working_memory` = pinned **charter** (frozen goal, written only by the goal authority) + **state** (executive-rewritten, safe-when-wrong); `anchoring` = pin (primacy) + tail (recency); `executive` = out-of-band state updater. Goal-authority seam = the world-model swap point for roleplay later.
> - **Ō£ģ M1ŌĆōM5** (prior commits 7335248eŌĆ”a65ae5d8): contracts + `session_templates` (tenancy: System owner NULL / Per-user `UNIQUE(owner_user_id,code)`) + `working_memory_seed`; templates CRUD + frozen-charter seed; anchoring text+voice (shared helper, EC-3/EC-7); `working_memory` block as SSOT in knowledge-service; the `executive` worker (state-only, `covered` monotonic, charter immutable by construction ŌĆö no `update_charter` exists). M5 **behaviorally proven** with real local Qwen2.5 7B.
> - **Ō£ģ M6 ŌĆö `/evaluate` scorecard (commit `53b832bd`).** `POST /v1/chat/sessions/{id}/evaluate` ŌĆö non-agentic pipeline scoring transcript + final `working_memory` + template rubric ŌåÆ structured `Scorecard` (STAR/clarity/filler/per-checklist/tips), stored as a `scorecard` ChatOutput. Coercion safe-when-wrong: per-checklist verdict rebuilt from `charter.checklist` (model can't drop/invent), scores clamped, `partial` server-set (EC-13). EC-10 no-model ŌåÆ 409. Portable (no `response_format` ŌĆö lm_studio quirk). 23 tests; chat-service suite 442 green.
> - **Ō£ģ M7 ŌĆö seed + frontend (commit `<pending>`).** 3 System templates seeded via the chat-service migration (idempotent `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` on the partial unique index; admin/platform path ŌĆö users clone, never mutate System). FE `features/interview/` (React-MVC): `useInterviewSetup`/`useEvaluation` hooks (controllers), `PersonaPicker`/`EndEvaluateBar`/`ScorecardView` views (render-only, props-driven), `InterviewPage` **reuses** the chat `ChatView` (text + voice, **no fork**; embedded mode, never unmounted) + scorecard overlay. Route `/interview` + sidebar entry (en/vi nav). 9 FE tests; typecheck/lint clean.
> - **Ō£ģ LIVE-SMOKE (cross-service, real Qwen2.5 7B):** templates list via API ŌåÆ start practice ŌåÆ real transcript ŌåÆ `POST /evaluate` ŌåÆ **201 in 7.7s**, scorecard checklist rebuilt to exactly the 4 charter items (model marked 2 covered / 2 not, matching the transcript), `partial=True`, persisted as a `scorecard` ChatOutput. M6 now proven-live, same bar as M5. (`scratchpad/m6_evaluate_smoke.py`.)
> - **Ō¢Č NEXT:** **POST-REVIEW #3 (human checkpoint) ŌĆö present + WAIT.** Then optional **browser smoke** (test account: `/interview` ŌåÆ pick persona ŌåÆ answer a couple turns ŌåÆ End & evaluate ŌåÆ scorecard). After approval: merge `feat/interview-roleplay` ŌåÆ main (PR). The chat-service image was rebuilt+recreated this session with M6+M7 code (live-verified in container).
> - **Deferred (tracked):** **`D-PROVIDER-STRUCTURED-OUTPUT`** (architecture C ŌĆö gate: out-of-scope, cross-service / new feature) ŌĆö provider-registry should NORMALIZE structured-output (canonical intent ŌåÆ per-provider dialect; prefer `json_schema`, accepted by BOTH OpenAI and lm_studio) instead of passthrough-ing raw `response_format` (which 400s `json_object` on lm_studio). Until then, LLM-JSON callers omit `response_format` + parse robustly (executive + evaluate both do). Target: a dedicated provider-registry branch. See memory `provider-structured-output-quirk`.

> - **Ō£ģ `D-KG-PASSAGE-BACKFILL` CLEARED 2026-06-23 (passage-creation gap, root-caused live):** running the full Dracula journey surfaced that **wiki produced 0 articles** (and enrichment 0 proposals) ŌĆö `skipped_no_grounding`, because the KG project had **0 `:Passage` nodes**. Root cause: passages are ingested by the `chapter.published` **event handler**, which SKIPS when no project/embedding exists yet ([handlers.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/events/handlers.py) lines 144/194); the **natural flow publishes chapters BEFORE the KG project + embedding model exist**, so the publish event fires too early and **nothing backfills** (`backfill-orders` only re-indexes existing passages). Proven via `index-drafts` (on-demand ingest ŌåÆ 8 passages where the journey made 0). **Fix:** `backfill_published_passages` ([passage_ingester.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/extraction/passage_ingester.py)) ingests passages for all PUBLISHED chapters as canon, wired best-effort into `PUT /embedding-model` ([extraction.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/routers/public/extraction.py) ŌĆö the moment passages become ingestable; idempotent; a model CHANGE re-ingests at the new dim). 24 ingester + 35 embedding-model + 76 passage/event/raw-search tests; provider-gate clean. **LIVE: natural flow (publishŌåÆprojectŌåÆembedding) now backfills 6 canon `:Passage` nodes** where it made 0 before (`verify_passage_backfill.py`). NOTE surfaced: `book_chapter_publish` is proposeŌåÆconfirm ŌĆö the journey drivers never confirmed it, so chapters stayed `draft` (extraction reads drafts; wiki needs published+grounded).
> - **Ō£ģ END-TO-END PAYOFF RE-VERIFIED 2026-06-23 (with a CONFIRMED publish):** fresh book `019ef35c` / project `019ef35e` ŌĆö natural flow (confirm-publish ŌåÆ create project ŌåÆ set embedding ŌåÆ backfill **6 passages** ŌåÆ KG build ŌåÆ wiki ŌåÆ enrich). **Wiki 0ŌåÆ20 articles ┬Ę Enrichment 0ŌåÆ3 proposals (job completed, 30,131 `actual_cost_tokens`) ┬Ę chapter written (2330 chars)** ŌĆö the two grounding-dependent steps that were 0 now produce real output. Also caught + fixed a stale `lore-enrichment-worker` (pre-rename image ŌåÆ `column actual_cost_usd does not exist`); recreating it fixed enrichment. **Deploy note: after a schema rename, recreate worker containers too, not just the API service.**
