# Session Handoff ŌĆö `feat/extraction-knowledge-architecture` ┬Ę **EXTRACTION RE-ARCH ŌĆö FND+RE+PROV+OBS SHIPPED; MERGE/PLAN/CACHE remain** ┬Ę (prior: design complete; `feat/glossary-assistant-coverage` ŌĆö 26/26, PR #39) ┬Ę 2026-06-22

> **Ō¢Č NEXT SESSION STARTS HERE ŌĆö extraction re-arch epic COMPLETE. ­¤Äē All 7 lanes + every follow-on shipped: FNDŌ£ģ REŌ£ģ PROVŌ£ģ OBSŌ£ģ MERGEŌ£ģ PLANŌ£ģ CACHE (slice1 cache-gate / 3a retention / 3b replay / 3c MinIO offload)Ō£ģ + `D-CACHE-PLANNER-WIRING` Part 1 (split-aware cost estimate)Ō£ģ + Part 2 (planner feasibility gate)Ō£ģ. `D-SDK-DISTRIBUTION-SPLIT` RESOLVED (PR #41 ŌåÆ main; planner ships in the rebuilt runtime ŌĆö live-verified in-container). Nothing extraction-epic remains ŌĆö even `D-EXTRACTION-FND-E2E-SMOKE` is closed (the unplannable-path live E2E ran green on the real stack; both translation images rebuilt `--no-cache`). NEXT = a NEW track: either the KG-ontology consolidated live E2E (the Ō¢Č block below) OR the architecture-audit Crit/High backlog (see `docs/analysis/`, memory `architecture-audit-2026-06-20`). Ask the user which.**
> - **Ō£ģ D-CACHE-PLANNER-WIRING Part 2 ŌĆö planner feasibility gate (commit `a37c0527`; DESIGN [2026-06-22-planner-executor-wiring-part2.md](../specs/2026-06-22-planner-executor-wiring-part2.md), Option B).** KEY REFRAME: `plan()` is a token-budget STRUCTURE planner (estimates, not text payloads) ŌåÆ it CAN'T replace the windower (which makes prose); its value is the `Unplannable` signal. So Part 2 wires it as a **pre-flight feasibility GATE** over the existing windowing, NOT a replacement: build a Unit per (window├Śbatch), run `plan()`, and any unit it refuses (an oversized block that can't fit even alone) ŌåÆ record a new `unplannable` batch-outcome + **skip its LLM call** (no truncation, no tokens) ŌåÆ chapter derives `completed_with_errors` (INV-F15) so the block is VISIBLE. The unit id IS the `{window_idx}:{batch_idx}` coordinate ŌćÆ **zero remap** onto the cache-gate/OBS/provenance seams (my earlier "structural XL" fear was wrong ŌĆö the gate touches none of them). Fires only when input won't fit with the minimum output reservation = exactly when the executor would truncate (no false-positive skip ŌĆö a flagged window couldn't extract anyway). Best-effort: any planner failure ŌåÆ ungated (prior path). New OBS taxonomy `unplannable` (distinct from `truncated`). VERIFY: 83 extraction unit tests + **FULL LIVE E2E** (`D-EXTRACTION-FND-E2E-SMOKE` ŌĆö see below); provider-gate clean.
> - **Ō£ģ D-EXTRACTION-FND-E2E-SMOKE (unplannable path) ŌĆö DONE live on the real stack.** Auth (test acct) ŌåÆ created a 348k-char single-block chapter in the Dracula book ŌåÆ `POST /v1/extraction/.../extract-glossary` ŌåÆ worker ŌåÆ **`extraction_batch_outcomes.status='unplannable'`** + chapter `completed_with_errors`, job done in <1s with **NO LLM call**. Clean before/after: the SAME job on the pre-gate image produced `llm_error` (the oversized window hit `LLMInvalidRequest` context-overflow ŌĆö the exact S1ŌĆōS5 failure); the gated worker produced `unplannable` (graceful skip). **GOTCHA caught (memory `docker-stale-copy-layer-false-smoke`):** the first rebuild served a STALE app COPY layer (old code, no gate) while the SDK layer was fresh ŌĆö so my initial "in-container `plan()` snippet" smoke was a FALSE pass (it tested the SDK, not the worker's gate). Fixed with `build --no-cache` + `up --force-recreate`; re-verified the gate code is in the container before re-running. Both translation images are now rebuilt `--no-cache` (current code live). Test chapter cleaned up. The broader full-pipeline-WITH-entities E2E (needs a live LLM producing entities) remains nice-to-have, not blocking.
> - **Ō£ģ CACHE D-CACHE-PLANNER-WIRING Part 1 ŌĆö split-aware cost estimate (commit `30c7549d`).** `estimate_extraction_cost` ([extraction_prompt.py](../../services/translation-service/app/workers/extraction_prompt.py)) now models each (chapter ├Ś kind-batch) as a `chunk`-splittable planner Unit (`max_units_per_call=1` so the per-unit input sum == real per-call input ŌĆö extraction injects window text PER batch call, never shared) and splits oversized chapters into windows, so the quote tracks the executor's real fan-out (+ surfaces `unplannable`/`model_fit_warning`). Extracted the worker's context resolver into shared [extraction_model.py](../../services/translation-service/app/workers/extraction_model.py); the HTTP route + MCP start-extraction tool resolve the REAL model context and thread it in (so the quote uses the SAME budget the executor will). ANY planner failure (ImportError or plan() error) degrades to the flat heuristic ŌĆö a cost estimate must never 500 job creation. VERIFY: 4 cost + worker 11 + mcp 36 + extraction subset 32 green; provider-gate clean.
> - **Ō£ģ CACHE Slice 3a ŌĆö retention (commit `36193284`, not pushed).** `purge_stale_raw_outputs` ([extraction_cache.py](../../services/translation-service/app/workers/extraction_cache.py)) keeps the latest K content-hash GENERATIONS per (owner, book, chapter) + purges older (cache growth bounded by edit-history depth, not edit count) + `POST /internal/translation/extraction-cache/retention` (the scheduler seam). Destructive-op safety: current gen is always rank-1 (never purged even at keepŌåÆfloored-1); rows join on content_hash so a gen is never half-purged; DENSE_RANK over-keeps on ties; partition keyed by owner_user_id (INV-9). VERIFY (live PG): keep-K / scope / tenant-isolation test; 66 extraction tests green; provider-gate clean.
> - **Ō£ģ CACHE Slice 3b ŌĆö replay (commit `0f097422`, not pushed).** New [extraction_replay.py](../../services/translation-service/app/workers/extraction_replay.py) `replay_chapter_from_cache` re-drives the glossary writeback from a chapter's CACHED parse at **$0 LLM** (recovery: failed writeback / cleared entities / re-materialize) + `POST /internal/translation/extraction-cache/replay`. Faithful-by-construction: proceeds only when CURRENT text still hashes to the cached `content_hash` AND the source job's profile still hashes to the cached `profile_hash` (else no_cache / profile_unavailable ŌåÆ fresh extraction) ŌĆö so re-stamped evidence offsets + links + attribute-actions are all valid. Gating: grant (EDIT on book, asserted-user re-verified), tenancy (reads ONLY caller's own cache, writes caller-attributed ŌĆö INV-9), confirm (confirm=False ŌåÆ dry-run PREVIEW no write; confirm=True ŌåÆ idempotent writeback w/ the worker's writeback_key). **/review-impl (self): 1 MED** (duplicated `_merge_window_entities` ŌåÆ import the worker's single source of truth ŌĆö guards a future merge-key drift) **+ 1 LOW** (writeback failure ŌåÆ distinct `writeback_failed` status). VERIFY (live PG): 5 replay tests (preview-no-write / confirm-writes-merged / content-drift / profile-unavailable / tenant-isolation) + 3 cache + extraction+dispatch subset green; provider-gate clean.
> - **Ō£ģ FND M0 ŌĆö `finish_reason` propagation (the blocker).** Verified the gateway already propagates it (streamerŌåÆaggregatorŌåÆ`result.finish_reason`, committed Apr; live-confirmed: real chat + extraction `llm_jobs` carry it in the column AND `result`). Wired the consumer: typed `Job.finish_reason` SDK accessor ([models.py](../../sdks/python/loreweave_llm/models.py)) + the worker reads it per batch, records `batch_finish_reasons`, **warns on `length` truncation** (the invisible 26-scenario data-loss signal). Tests: SDK 2 + worker 2.
> - **Ō£ģ FND M1 ŌĆö concurrency + two-ledger (the 4-HIGH "concurrency" finding).** Migration **0032_extraction_concurrency** ([extraction_concurrency.go](../../services/glossary-service/internal/migrate/extraction_concurrency.go)): `normalized_name` (GENERATED off trigger-maintained `cached_name`) + `uq_entity_dedup` partial unique index + `uq_evidence_dedup` + `extraction_writeback_log`. [`extraction_handler.go`](../../services/glossary-service/internal/api/extraction_handler.go) `bulkExtractEntities` rewritten: per-book `pg_advisory_xact_lock` + **whole-chapter transaction** + `writeback_key` idempotency (replay=no-op) + content-hash stored + **idempotent evidence** (`ON CONFLICT`) + transactional outbox (threaded `pgxRWQuerier` through 4 helpers; mcp_server + perlang test callers updated). Worker computes `content_hash`/`writeback_key` + a **source-drift precondition** (re-fetch+compare ŌåÆ skip stale) + passes the two-ledger fields ([extraction_worker.py](../../services/translation-service/app/workers/extraction_worker.py), [glossary_client.py](../../services/translation-service/app/workers/glossary_client.py)).

<details><summary>Design-complete handoff (FND now built ŌĆö kept for context)</summary>

> **Ō¢Č extraction/LLM-pipeline re-architecture, BUILD via the parallel plan.** Branch `feat/extraction-knowledge-architecture` (off the glossary work). The 26-scenario test surfaced 5 architectural gaps; this session did the **full design**, now COMPLETE and build-ready:
> - **Design docs (committed):** [architecture rev 2](../specs/2026-06-21-extraction-pipeline-architecture.md) (┬¦7ŌĆō┬¦11 = a 4-lens adversarial evaluation: concurrency/failure/scale/tenancy, with the scenario matrix + hardening contracts), [reasoning-effort](../specs/2026-06-21-reasoning-effort-control.md), [**detailed design**](../specs/2026-06-21-extraction-pipeline-detailed-design.md) (every open question RESOLVED ŌĆö decisions table ┬¦1 + DDL ┬¦2 + interfaces ┬¦3 + 15 enforceable invariants ┬¦4), [**parallel plan**](../plans/2026-06-21-extraction-pipeline-plan.md).
> - **The 5 gaps ŌåÆ one missing layer:** an explicit `PLAN ŌåÆ EXECUTE+CACHE ŌåÆ VALIDATE ŌåÆ WRITEBACK ŌåÆ OBSERVE` spine. P1 observability (services exist, unwired), P2 unified planner (scattered across 5+ modules), P3 evidence provenance (columns exist, unpopulated), P4 merge/append policy (no append, silent skips), P5 raw cache (spec'd, was deferred).
> - **Key design verdicts to honor in BUILD:** the **two-ledger model** (raw-output cache ŌēĀ writeback-coverage log ŌĆö else a failed writeback permanently masks un-landed entities = data-loss amplifier); **concurrency contract** (glossary has NO `UNIQUE(book,kind,name)` today ŌåÆ concurrent jobs dup; need constraint + per-book advisory lock + transactional whole-chapter writeback + idempotency key + content-hash precondition); **batch-outcome taxonomy** (empty_valid ŌēĀ validation_rejected ŌēĀ truncated ŌĆö the literal 26-scenario bug); **two-phase planner** (split-down, not just pack-up; extraction has no chapter-chunking); **provenance trust** (model offsets are INV-6 untrusted, validate not trust); **verified-clobber guard** (merge never overwrites human-verified); **tenancy** (owner_user_id + grant gate + INV-6 + redaction on every new table/event). 4 HIGH findings are design-blocking ŌåÆ resolved in the detailed design.
> - **Build = 7 parallel lanes (plan ┬¦2):** FND (M0 finish_reason ŌåÆ M1 concurrency/two-ledger ŌĆö the blocker), RE (reasoning-effort + the wiring no-op fix), PLAN (planner SDK), OBS, PROV, MERGE, CACHE. Critical path FNDŌåÆPROVŌåÆMERGE; RE+PLAN start at minute 0. `extraction_handler.go` is the hot file ŌĆö FNDŌåÆPROVŌåÆMERGE sequence on it, never concurrent.
> - **Gate decision (PO-reversible):** Option A ŌĆö pipeline-first, build in translation+glossary now, cache behind an interface (physical re-home to knowledge deferred = `D-EXTRACTION-REHOME-KNOWLEDGE`). 3 tracked deferrals: that + `D-GLOSSARY-MULTIROW-ATTR-VALUES` + `D-RAWCACHE-MINIO-OFFLOAD`.
> - **First BUILD action next session:** start lane **FND** (M0 finish_reason propagation, then M1) + lane **RE** in parallel. The RE wiring fix (chat thinking toggle is a live no-op today) is a standalone user-visible win.

</details>

# Ō¢Č Dracula end-to-end JOURNEY verification (live, 2026-06-22) ŌĆö steps 1ŌĆō8 PROVEN; 9ŌĆō11 remain

> **The real product scenario, driven live on the actual Dracula book** (`019eeb09`, test account) instead of clearing micro-deferrals. Journey: create book ŌåÆ agent web-search+suggest ontology ŌåÆ approve ŌåÆ import ŌåÆ extract glossary ŌåÆ translate glossary+chapter to vi ŌåÆ build KG ŌåÆ build wiki ŌåÆ enrich ŌåÆ write chapter.
> - **Ō£ģ 1 Create book / Ō£ģ 2 Agent-suggested ontology (book-optimized: `vampire/hunter/victim/werewolf/witch/artifact` + defaults) / Ō£ģ 3 Approve (12 kinds, 52 attrs adopted) / Ō£ģ 4 Import (**4 published chapters now** ŌĆö fetched the full public-domain novel from Gutenberg #345 since the repo ships only ch01, split it, bulk-ingested ch2ŌĆō4 via `POST /v1/books/{id}/chapters/bulk`; a stray `OVERSIZED unplannable E2E` draft sits at sort 99) / Ō£ģ 5 Extract glossary (30 entities) / Ō£ģ 6 GlossaryŌåÆvi (233 attr translations) / Ō£ģ 7 ChapterŌåÆvi (`vi/completed`).** All verified by live DB state, not handoff claims.
> - **Ō£ģ 8 Build KG ŌĆö DONE this session (was the key blocker).** Drove `POST /v1/knowledge/projects/{id}/extraction/{estimate,start}` live. **Neo4j now holds the Dracula graph: 65 Entity (Count Dracula, Jonathan Harker, Castle Dracula, Carpathians, LondonŌĆ”), 17 Event, 3 Fact, 10 Passage, 15 ExtractionSource + 60 EVIDENCED_BY + 21 RELATES_TO** (job complete, 65 items, $0.06, gemma + bge-m3, scope=all). **­¤ö┤ BLOCKER SURFACED (real UX gap ŌåÆ fix): build-KG is gated behind the K17.9 embedding benchmark, which had NEVER been run here** (0 benchmark runs). The non-obvious trip: `min_runs: 3` ŌĆö a `runs=1` benchmark returns `passed:false` DESPITE perfect recall@3=1.0/mrr=1.0 (bge-m3 passes cleanly at runs=3; 1024-dim neg-control override 0.70 applied). The agent's `kg_build_graph` mints a token fine but extraction 409s `benchmark_missing` until the user benchmarks their embedding model ŌĆö the journey never surfaces this prerequisite. Also: changing the project embedding model returns `extraction_status:disabled` + deletes prior nodes (vector-space reset). **`D-JOURNEY-KG-BENCHMARK-UX`** (tracked; ANALYSIS DONE ŌåÆ [docs/specs/2026-06-22-kg-benchmark-ux-gap-analysis.md](../specs/2026-06-22-kg-benchmark-ux-gap-analysis.md)). **Verdict: primarily a DESIGN gap (an unreconciled contradiction) + 2 implementation gaps.** The benchmark WAS designed as a visible "Run benchmark" step gating Confirm (RAID brief `05_build-graph-gates.md`, UX review P0 KN-1/BL-16) and the FE wires that button ŌĆö but: **(a) DESIGN contradiction** ŌĆö the gate keys benchmark to `(user, project, embedding_model)` (extraction.py:696) and the FE button is on the REAL project, yet the runner REFUSES any project that already has chapter/chat/glossary passages (`runner.py:223-227` `not_benchmark_project`); all three can't hold once the project has content, so on a non-trivial project the UI's only remedy structurally can't clear the gate (the RAID brief scoped "NO backend changes" assuming the endpoint fit ŌĆö it didn't). I only cleared it because change-embedding had just emptied the project. **(b) IMPL** ŌĆö `core.py:159` short-circuits `runs<min_runs` BEFORE metrics ŌåÆ `runs=1` returns `passed:false` at `recall@3=1.0` with no reason + a misleading FE "low-quality results" badge (FE always sends runs:3, so only API/agent callers hit it ŌĆö i.e. exactly my drive). **(c) DESIGN** ŌĆö a successful run leaves ~10 synthetic `benchmark_entity` passages, no cleanup (pollution). **(d) IMPL** ŌĆö `kg_build_graph` warns but there's NO `kg_run_benchmark` MCP tool ŌåÆ agent dead-end. **Fix breakdown:** R1 decouple benchmarkŌåÆ(user,embedding_model) on an auto-sandbox (root cause, needs a plan + contract change); R2 add `fail_reason` + stop FE mislabelling a perfect short run; R3 clamp/reject `runs<min_runs` on the interactive endpoint; R4 add `kg_run_benchmark` MCP tool (MCP-first). R2+R3 are fix-now-small; R1+R4 need a plan. **Ō¢Č BATCH-FIX BUILD (plan: [2026-06-22-kg-benchmark-ux-fix.md](../plans/2026-06-22-kg-benchmark-ux-fix.md)) ŌĆö BACKEND M1ŌĆōM3 DONE 2026-06-22 (commits 7f3502af, ccf52484, 0d8306e2, 17c89fa0):** **M1/R1** re-keyed the gate to `(user, embedding_model)` (`BenchmarkRunsRepo.get_latest_for_model`) + routed the run to a hidden per-(user,model) sandbox (`get_or_create_benchmark_sandbox`; migration `is_benchmark_sandbox`; list-excluded) ŌĆö **live-proven:** ran the benchmark on the content-bearing Dracula project (65 entities) ŌåÆ passed, **NO `not_benchmark_project`** (old code 409s), sandbox created with the fixtures, real project's `benchmark_entity` count stayed 10 (**zero new pollution**), sandbox absent from list API. **M2/R2+R3** `gate_failures()` named codes (`insufficient_runs` ŌēĀ a quality fail) threaded to the response/to_json + the runner clamps `runs` up to `min_runs` ŌĆö **live-proven:** `runs=1` ŌåÆ `passed=True, runs=3, gate_failures=[]` (the perfect-but-failed trap is gone). **M3/R4** `kg_run_benchmark` MCP tool (handler + arg model + definition + shim; OWNER-only, direct, runs on the sandbox) ŌĆö catalogue unit-green (TOOL_DEFINITIONS 26). Full knowledge unit suite **2988 green**; provider-gate clean. **REMAINING: M4 (FE) + M5 (contract)** ŌĆö R2 badge copy (`insufficient_runs` ŌåÆ "inconclusive, needs Ōēź3 runs" not "low-quality"), R5 `BuildGraphDialog` prerequisite checklist, R6 model-swap warning copy + the OpenAPI `gate_failures`/tool additions. The FE is isolated copy/checklist on a now-working backend; the journey's KG-build dead-end is ALREADY cleared by the backend.
> - **Ō£ģ 9 Build wiki ŌĆö DONE 2026-06-22.** Drove `POST /internal/knowledge/books/{id}/wiki/generate` (S2S) for 3 entities ŌåÆ **3 wiki articles generated** (Jonathan Harker / Count Dracula / Mina; 2.3ŌĆō5KB bodies, `generation_status=needs_review` from the local-model verify pass), job complete, $0.15 via gemma. **­¤ö┤ GATE SURFACED: wiki-gen is flag-gated OFF** (`WIKI_GEN_ENABLED=false` ŌåÆ the `wiki_gen_processor` consumer never runs, job sits `pending` forever); had to restart knowledge-service with `KNOWLEDGE_WIKI_GEN_ENABLED=true`. Another un-surfaced journey prerequisite (like the KG benchmark) ŌåÆ **`D-JOURNEY-WIKI-FLAG` Ō£ģ FIXED 2026-06-22 (961536c9):** the consumer now ALWAYS runs (it's idle/free until a user-triggered, cost-gated job arrives ŌĆö gating it off-by-default only produced silent eternal pends); spend stays bounded per-request (`max_spend_usd` + the cost-gated trigger), not a platform env. `wiki_gen_enabled` deprecatedŌåÆdefault True, no longer gates the consumer. **Live-proven:** restarted knowledge-service with `WIKI_GEN_ENABLED=false` ŌåÆ the wiki-gen consumer still started.
> - **Ō£ģ 10 Enrichment ŌĆö DROVE IT + FIXED 2026-06-22 (961536c9).** `POST /v1/lore-enrichment/projects/{id}/auto-enrich` ŌåÆ scanned 30 entities, detected 30 gaps. **The user's call was right ŌĆö gemma is fine, the CODE was weak; 3 code bugs found + fixed (NOT a model problem):** **(A) `D-JOURNEY-ENRICH-EMPTY-COMPLETION`** ŌĆö one empty LLM completion raised `CompletionSeamError` that escaped the per-gap handler and **FAILED the whole job**, discarding the gaps that worked. Now caught as a per-gap skip (runner.py). **(C) `D-JOURNEY-ENRICH-VERBATIM`** ŌĆö the gen prompt said "use ONLY the excerpts"+"don't invent" but never "synthesize", so gemma safely **copied the source verbatim**, which our OWN anti-plagiarism gate then auto-rejected (self-defeating loop); both language templates now demand paraphrase/re-creation (generate.py). **(B) `D-JOURNEY-ENRICH-COST-UNITS`** ŌĆö `spent=8409` is **8409 TOKENS not $8409** (the cap is token-denominated per the C1 PO ruling, but columns/logs say `*_cost_usd`); relabelled the log, the DB/API `*_usd`ŌåÆtokens rename is structural (migration+FE) and tracked. **Live-proven (re-ran after the fix):** job **COMPLETED** (was `failed`), **4 proposals `review_status='proposed'` NOT `rejected`** ŌĆö gemma now PARAPHRASES (Borgo Pass Õ£░ńÉå"Ķ┐×µÄźµ»öµ¢»ńē╣ķćīĶī©õĖÄÕĖāń¦æń╗┤ń║│ńÜäÕ▒▒ķŚ┤ĶĘ»ÕŠä" ŌĆö synthesized, not the verbatim English source), clearing the gate. Confidence 0.30 is the H0 quarantine floor (awaiting human review), not a rejection.
> - **Ō£ģ 11 Write new chapter ŌĆö DRIVEN AGENT-SIDE 2026-06-22 via the new `composition_generate` MCP tool.** Through the gateway: `composition_create_work` ŌåÆ `composition_outline_node_create` (chapter+scene; both kinds require a real book `chapter_id` per the `outline_chapter_required` constraint, so a new book chapter `019eef4c` was created first) ŌåÆ `composition_generate` (propose, mints a `composition.generate` token) ŌåÆ confirm (`/v1/composition/actions/confirm`) ŌåÆ the confirm effect ran `engine.generate_chapter(persist=True)` IN-PROCESS ŌåÆ **gemma drafted a full gothic chapter (real Stoker-voice prose) ŌåÆ PERSISTED to the book draft (draft_version 2), status `completed`.** (`grounding=False`/`canon=skipped_no_cast` because the minimal scene carried no `present_entity_ids` ŌåÆ voice-only grounding; a scene with cast would pull KG/glossary canon.) **The grounded cowrite ENGINE is now agent-reachable.** Step 10 was likewise re-driven agent-side via the new `lore_enrichment_auto_enrich` MCP tool (job `019eef45` ŌåÆ 2 synthesized `proposed` proposals: Borgo Pass / Bistritz, paraphrased not verbatim).
> - **Scenario status:** the 2 tool-gaps are closed and BOTH new tools are real-execution-proven agent-driven on the Dracula book. **FULL FRESH-BOOK AGENT-DRIVEN RUN done 2026-06-22** (fresh book `019eef55ŌĆ”`, KG project `019eef5dŌĆ”`, all through the gateway MCP surface unless noted):
>   - Ō£ģ **1 create** (`book_create`) ┬Ę Ō£ģ **2 ontology** (`glossary_web_search` + baseline adopted + `vampire`+`hunter` custom kinds via `glossary_propose_new_kind`ŌåÆglossary confirm; verified in `book_kinds`) ┬Ę Ō£ģ **3 import** (`book_chapter_bulk_create` real Dracula ch.1 + publish) ┬Ę Ō£ģ **4 extract** (`translation_start_extraction` proposeŌåÆconfirm ŌåÆ **26 entities**) ┬Ę Ō£ģ **5 translateŌåÆvi** (`translation_start_job` proposeŌåÆconfirm ŌåÆ completed).
>   - Ō£ģ **6 KG build** ŌĆö `kg_build_graph` proposeŌåÆ`/v1/kg/actions/confirm` ŌåÆ **26 Entity nodes in Neo4j** for project `019eef5dŌĆ”`, `extraction_status=ready` (benchmark via REST `benchmark-run` PASSED, embedding model via PATCH ŌĆö config steps with no MCP surface). **­¤ö┤ NEW GATEWAY GAP `D-GW-XPROJECT-NOT-FORWARDED`:** project-scoped KG tools resolve the project from `X-Project-Id`, but **the ai-gateway federation DROPS `X-Project-Id`** ŌåÆ through the gateway they 'no project in scope'; DIRECT to knowledge `/mcp` (X-Project-Id set) they mint fine. So KG/wiki were driven **direct on knowledge `/mcp`** (still MCP tool-calls; the new composition/enrich tools are immune ŌĆö they take `project_id`/ids as explicit args). Re-confirmed `D-JOURNEY-KG-BENCHMARK-UX` (fresh project ŌåÆ embedding set ŌåÆ `extraction_status=disabled` until the benchmark runs; `kg_run_benchmark` MCP tool absent from the deployed gateway catalog).
>   - ŌŚÉ **7 wiki** ŌĆö `kg_build_wiki` propose mints + the confirm reaches the effect, but **422 "no entities ŌĆ” extract the glossary first"**. **ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ ROOT CAUSE CORRECTED 2026-06-22 + FIXED (M2 above, `D-WIKI-ENTITY-FREQ-GATE`):** this was NOT draft-vs-active. The `known-entities` Go handler IGNORES `status` and gates on `COUNT(chapter_links) >= min_frequency` (default **2**); the wiki effect called it at the default, so a single-chapter book (every entity freq 1) returned 0. Proven live: all 26 are `draft`, yet `status=active&min_frequency=1` ŌåÆ 26 and `&min_frequency=2` ŌåÆ 0. (The OLD Dracula book worked because it has 4 chapters ŌåÆ freq Ōēź2, not because its entities were active.) Wiki resolver now passes `min_frequency=1`.
>   - Ō£ģ **8 enrich** (`lore_enrichment_auto_enrich` ŌĆö the NEW tool, **through the gateway**) ŌåÆ real synthesized proposal on the fresh book once KG embeddings existed (e.g. Borgo Pass Õ£░ńÉå/features, `review_status=proposed`). First pass before KG produced 0 (retrieval needs the book embeddings the KG build creates). ┬Ę Ō£ģ **9 write** (`composition_generate` ŌĆö the NEW tool ŌĆö proposeŌåÆconfirm ŌåÆ **real gothic chapter generated + persisted to the fresh book draft, draft_version 2**).
>   - **Verdict (original run): 8/9 driven; the 2 blockers (X-Project-Id drop + wiki freq-gate) were then ROOT-CAUSED + FIXED in the batch above, and the journey RE-RUN clean 9/9 through the gateway (see the Ō£ģ PAYOFF RUN line in the NEXT block).** Non-gateway-MCP steps in the original run were config-only setup (embedding-model pick, benchmark) + the KG/wiki tools (then blocked by `D-GW-XPROJECT-NOT-FORWARDED`, now forwarded). **All four agent tools (`kg_build_graph`, `kg_build_wiki`, `lore_enrichment_auto_enrich`, `composition_generate`) now work cleanly through the gateway end-to-end.**

Now: every kind gets 3-6 attributes, every attribute gets a specific description, curate-adopted-defaults guidance, and the `glossary_book_patch` FLAT arg-shape (weak models defaulted to the entity-edit `changes` diff ŌåÆ silent no-op). Live-verified proactive (hunter: methods/allegiance/notable_kills/motivation, each described).
>   - **­¤ö┤ GUI approve gap FIXED (the headline) ŌĆö `D-GLOSSARY-AUTOCONFIRM`** ŌĆö the local model mints the confirm_token but never calls the frontend `glossary_confirm_action` tool, so NO Approve card rendered (only prose "confirm in your dashboard"); a GUI-only user couldn't approve. The FE rendered the card ONLY from a suspended frontend tool. Fix: `AssistantMessage` now AUTO-renders the confirm card from any completed propose result carrying a LIVE confirm_token (reuses ConfirmCard/ConfirmActionCard, synthetic record, resume no-ops, exp-decoded to skip stale replay, deduped vs explicit). Plus the live SSE path now keeps the tool `result` so the card shows during the turn, not only after reload. (Caught only because the user insisted on a REAL in-GUI test ŌĆö prior API-POST approvals had masked it.)

F3c stay-on-task guard kills the web-search/list-templates tangent.

New migration **0035_system_attr_descriptions** ([system_attr_descriptions.go](../../services/glossary-service/internal/migrate/system_attr_descriptions.go), registered in [ledger.go](../../services/glossary-service/internal/migrate/ledger.go)): an idempotent, non-clobbering `UPDATE system_attributes` over the spec's 81 authored `(kind_code, attr_code)` cells (name/term display keys omitted) that sets `description` + recomputes `content_hash` with the EXACT inline md5 the seed/`attrContentHash` uses ŌĆö so the new hash equals a fresh seed-with-description (the parity adopt + G5 Sync depend on). `COALESCE(TRIM(description),'')=''` guard ŌćÆ admin edits survive + re-run = 0 rows. **Decision honored: sync-only, NO backfill** ŌĆö new books carry descriptions at adopt; existing books pull via `sync_apply take_theirs` (`keep_mine` preserves a local edit). Authored table re-verified against `domain.DefaultKinds` (exact 81/93). **LIVE-SMOKE (real dev DB `loreweave_glossary`, rebuilt+restarted glossary-service ŌåÆ RunChain applied 0035 on actual startup):** `schema_migrations` now has `0035`; `system_attributes` 0ŌåÆ**81** descriptions (name/term still 0); `character.aliases` hash moved `0be5469aŌĆ”`ŌåÆ`5ae3679eŌĆ”`; and **22,736 existing system-sourced book_attributes now drift** (`source_hash ŌēĀ` updated System `content_hash`) across 303 books ŌĆö the exact surface `glossary_book_sync_available` lists, so existing books pull via Sync. **End-to-end on a REAL book via the gateway:** on `019ee969` (õĖćÕÅżńź×ÕĖØ) the extraction-profile started at **0/60** descriptions; `GET ŌĆ”/sync/available` listed **51** attribute updates; `POST ŌĆ”/sync/apply take_theirs` applied all 51; the profile then read **51/60** with `character.aliases: Other names, titles, epithetsŌĆ”` ŌĆö the authored text now flowing through the worker's cross-service extraction-profile. VERIFY (real PG 5555, throwaway DBs): migrate-pkg mechanics test (93 emptyŌåÆ81 set + 12 name/term untouched; hash moves + equals seed-formula; idempotent; non-clobber) + api-pkg propagation test (adopt-before-edit ŌåÆ `sync/available` lists attrs with authored `theirs` ŌåÆ take_theirs pulls / keep_mine preserves; new-adopt carries descriptions immediately; idempotent count=2 on shared DB) ŌĆö both green; full glossary module `-p1` green (api/events/migrate/sanitize/shortdesc); gofmt + vet + ai-provider-gate clean. **M3 extraction-prompt smoke not run** (the adoptŌåÆ`book_attributes.description` landing ŌĆö exactly what `extraction_prompt.py` reads ŌĆö is proven by the api test; a full worker e2e rides `D-EXTRACTION-FND-E2E-SMOKE`).

# Session Handoff ŌĆö `feat/glossary-assistant-coverage` ┬Ę **GLOSSARY TRACK COMPLETE + ALL 26 SCENARIOS VERIFIED (2026-06-21)** ┬Ę **merged `main` 2026-06-21 (MCP fan-out epic)** ┬Ę 2026-06-21

> **Ō¢Č SCENARIO TEST ŌĆö 26/26 COMPLETE (2026-06-21, local Gemma-4-26B-QAT, book `019ee969`).** Finished the full S1ŌĆōS26 sweep on a healthy stack. Results table in [`docs/test/2026-06-21-glossary-assistant-scenario-checklist.md`](../test/2026-06-21-glossary-assistant-scenario-checklist.md). **This session drove the 14 untested scenarios:** the 5 reads (S16 evidence / S17 chapter-links / S18 unknowns / S19 merge-candidates / S21 revisions), S14 create-evidence (W) + S15 create-chapter-link (W) direct writes, **S2** book kind+attr create (DB-verified `faction`/`leader`, book-scoped), **S3** `glossary_book_set_kind_genres` delta-add xianxia (DB-verified), **S8** re-extract confirmŌåÆApplyŌåÆjobŌåÆcompleted (0 entities this run = local-model malformed-JSON artifact, pipeline graceful), **S12** restore-revision confirmŌåÆApply (`restored:true`), **S22** admin (assistant called 0 tools + refused; `TestAdminMCP_*` suite green: transport-gate 401 / absent-from-user-catalog / RS256 confirm / user-token-rejected), **S23** grant-deny (18 ownership/grant tests green inc. `MutatingRoutesRejectViewGrantee` + `*FailsClosed`), **S24** injection (planted hostile evidence ŌåÆ assistant flagged it as a prompt-injection, called no merge/delete, no PWNED ŌĆö INV-6), **S25** tri-lingual no-confusion (Vietnamese convo + Chinese source + English target ŌåÆ `propose_aliases language_code=en`). Earlier this branch: S1/S4a/S5/S6/S7/S9/S10/S11/S13/S20/S26. **S8 token-truncation bug ŌĆö `D-EXTRACTION-TOKEN-TRUNCATION`, FIXED + live-re-verified (don't blame the model):** the S8 "0 entities" was NOT a weak model ŌĆö direct LM Studio replay proved **output-token truncation**: `plan_kind_batches` budgeted only the input schema, packing **7 kinds into one call** whose output hit `finish_reason=length` at `max_tokens=12000` ŌåÆ cut mid-array ŌåÆ 0 parseable entities. Fix: cap `MAX_KINDS_PER_BATCH=3` (root cause ŌĆö bounds per-call output), raise `max_tokens` 12000ŌåÆ20000, and repair truncated/fenced arrays instead of discarding them ([extraction_prompt.py](../../services/translation-service/app/workers/extraction_prompt.py) + [extraction_worker.py](../../services/translation-service/app/workers/extraction_worker.py)); +5 regression tests. **Live:** same chapter now extracts 9 created + 5 skipped across 3 clean `finish=stop` batches (glossary 10ŌåÆ19). Grammar-constrained decoding tried but a permissive schema ran away ŌåÆ tracked `D-EXTRACTION-CONSTRAINED-DECODING` (needs tight per-kind schema). **1 LOW finding ŌĆö `D-BOOKPATCH-GENRE-ERRMSG`, FIXED + live-re-verified:** `glossary_book_patch` returned a misleading "no live row with that code" when an attribute exists under a different genre (genre is identity, not a patchable field). The attribute-level not-found branch now explains the `(kind, genre, code)` identity + the delete+recreate path ([book_tools.go](../../services/glossary-service/internal/api/book_tools.go)) + a regression test in `TestBookTool_PatchAttributeLevel`; glossary-service rebuilt + redeployed; on re-drive the agent got the new message and **self-corrected** (called `glossary_book_delete` to delete+recreate rather than the old 3-retry dead-end).

> **Ō¢Č LIVE SCENARIO TEST (2026-06-21, fresh stack @ HEAD, local Gemma-4 26B QAT via LM Studio).** Built a fresh fixture (book `019ee969` "õĖćÕÅżńź×ÕĖØ Scenario Test", 4 real chapters of õĖćÕÅżńź×ÕĖØ, adopted xianxia ontology: 2 genres / 9 kinds / 59 attrs) on claude-test; drove the glossary assistant via the chat API (gemma user_model, priced $0.10/$0.40 per Mtok). **Gemma is a RELIABLE tool-caller** (better than the old local 35B). **Verified live:** S0 federation (book_listŌåÆglossary_book_ontology_readŌåÆgrounded), **S7 extraction** (9 correct entities: Õ╝ĀĶŗźÕ░ś/µ▒ĀńæČ chars, ńÄēµ╝▒Õ«½ loc, ńź×µŁ”ÕŹ░Ķ«░ item, ń¼¼õĖĆõĖŁÕż«ÕĖØÕøĮ org), **S4 translation** (propose_translation ŌåÆ Õ╝ĀĶŗźÕ░ś=Zhang Ruochen etc., draft), **S6 per-language aliases** (propose_aliases ŌåÆ `["Zhang the Sword Saint","Master of the Divine Mark"]` JSON-array draft ŌĆö the exact chip-editor/composePerLanguageAliases format), **S11 status-change** (class-C card mints AND full round-trip: glossary `/actions/confirm` ŌåÆ draftŌåÆactive, updated=2), **S9 merge** (full round-trip: previewŌåÆApplyŌåÆloser soft-deleted + journal_id, winner kept), **S10 reassign_kind** (preview surfaces the accurate DATA-LOSS warning: 10 char-attrs dropped on characterŌåÆorganization), **S1 create** + **S13 edit** (both correctly SUSPEND for human Apply ŌĆö INV-1, no premature write), reads (ai_suggestions/jobs/search/ontology/get_entity), settings_list_models. ~17 distinct scenarios/tools green; both confirm paths (translation + glossary) proven end-to-end. **2 M3 BUGS CAUGHT + FIXED (commit `e3a03779`, unpushed):** (1) the MCP extraction path enqueued an EMPTY extraction_profile ŌåÆ 0 batches ŌåÆ 0 entities + a $0 cost estimate (agent omits the profile; now DERIVED from the book's auto-selected ontology in both the mint estimate and the shared core); (2) `model_source` hardcoded `platform_model` ŌåÆ a user_model ref 404'd as LLMModelNotFound (now `user_model`, caller-pays). Regression test + 5/5 extraction tests pass. **S5 deep-research ŌĆö LIVE-PROVEN (2026-06-21).** The external `local-web-search-service` (sibling repo, SearXNG-backed, ┬¦8-conformant, keyless) is live on `host.docker.internal:15487`. fixed by recreating the container. **(1) [HIGH]** the BFF didn't proxy `/v1/book/actions/*` (it matches `/v1/books` plural; confirm_action builds singular `/v1/book/actions`) **or** `/v1/settings/actions/*` (no `/v1/settings` proxy) ŌåÆ book+settings confirm round-trips 404'd at the gateway; fixed with two thin passthrough proxies in [`gateway-setup.ts`](../../services/api-gateway-bff/src/gateway-setup.ts) (mirror the C21 worlds pattern). **(2) [HIGH]** translation `/v1/translation/actions/*` was **internal-token-gated** (the FE Apply sends the user JWT ŌåÆ 401), unlike book/glossary/composition which are JWT-gated; switched to `get_current_user` + a bound-user assertion (`claims.u == JWT caller`) mirroring book-service. **(3) [MED]** book-scoped chats advertise the legacy glossary-only `glossary_confirm_action` beside the generic `confirm_action`, so the model can confirm a book action via the glossary tool ŌåÆ FE renders the glossary `ConfirmCard` ŌåÆ posts to `/v1/glossary/actions/confirm` ŌåÆ 422 (fails *closed*, breaks the round-trip); fixed in the FE by routing the confirm card **by descriptor domain** (dotted `book.publish` ŌåÆ generic `ConfirmActionCard`; non-dotted glossary descriptors ŌåÆ legacy `ConfirmCard`) + `ConfirmActionCard` now derives the domain from the descriptor when `args.domain` is absent. **VERIFY (all live on the running stack):** all 5 confirm domains now route at the BFF (book/settings 404ŌåÆ422, translation 401ŌåÆ400 ŌĆö all reaching their service with the user JWT); Book-native rows carry `source_ref=NULL`; attribute create enforces the **book-local FK guard** (kind_id/genre_id must be live rows of THIS book ŌåÆ 422, blocks a cross-book ref the raw FK would accept). Contract extended (`book-ontology` tag, 11 paths incl. retroactive adopt/ontology, 11 schemas, `Forbidden` resp). Single-service change (glossary + contracts) ŌåÆ no cross-service live-smoke token needed. /review-impl-grade self-review folded a `requireBookManage` extraction (killed 7 `_=userID` + 4 duplicated preambles).

All 3 levels ŌĆö genre/kind/attribute (+ kindŌåögenre links unioned). No new contract (ownerŌēłcaller under the Manage gate; the person setting up uses their own customizations ŌĆö arguably more correct than strict owner-scoping). `source_ref='user:<id>'` distinguishes the provenance; user genres/attrs carry their `content_hash`, user kinds get an inline md5 (no content_hash col); `is_hidden=NOT is_active`, `sort_order=0` (user_kinds has no sort_order). VERIFY: new `TestBookAdopt_UserTierShadowsSystem` (xianxia+universal genres + character kind + a 'name' attr all user-customized ŌåÆ user-sourced; romance not customized ŌåÆ system-sourced) + existing adopt/CRUD suites green; full `go test ./internal/api -p 1` 22.9s; gofmt/provider-gate clean.
> - **­¤ö© G4 ŌĆö DESTRUCTIVE CUTOVER DONE (this session, user-approved big-bang; built via a 9-agent Workflow + human foundation/verify).** The entity layer now lives on the BOOK tier. **Migration** (`migrate.go`): `UpGlossaryCutoverG4` (TRUNCATE entities CASCADE; repoint `glossary_entities.kind_id`ŌåÆ`book_kinds`, `entity_attribute_values.attr_def_id`ŌåÆ`book_attributes`; rewrite `recalculate_entity_snapshot` to book tier, source `'book'`) + `UpMergeCandidatesG4` (merge_candidates.kind_idŌåÆbook_kinds) + `UpGlossaryCutoverG4Cache` (restore cached_name/search_vector maintenance on the book-tier snapshot) + `UpGlossaryDropLegacyG4` (DROP `genre_groups`, `system_kind_attributes`, `system_kinds`/`user_kinds`.`genre_tags`). Wired LAST in main.go + the test chains. **Seed rewrite:** `SeedGenreKindAttr` now derives system_genres/kind_genres/attributes from `domain.DefaultKinds` in Go (no longer reads the dropped table/column). **Handlers retargeted to book-local:** extraction (loadKindMap/loadAttrDefMap[universal-genre DISTINCT ON]/createExtractedEntity/mergeExtractedEntity/findEntityByNameOrAlias/getKnownEntities/writeExtractionProfile[genre_groupsŌåÆbook_active_genres]), entity detail, glossary_translate, internalTranslationGlossary, listKinds (ŌåÆsystem_kind_genres/system_attributes), and **O2** ŌĆö `confirmSchema`/`createKindFromParams`/`createAttrDefFromParams` now mint into the **BOOK** tier (token-bound bookID, book-scoped, `errNotAdopted`ŌåÆ422; a user's assistant can no longer mutate the shared catalogue). Legacy per-book `genre_groups` routes/handlers RETIRED (`genres_crud.go`/`genres_handler.go`/`domain/genres.go` deleted). **E3 KG clear:** `reset_glossary_anchors` added to knowledge-service (manual maintenance fn, not auto-run). **Precondition:** a book MUST be adopted before entities can be created in it. **VERIFY (human-run, not trusted from agents):** full `go test ./... -p 1 -count=1` GREEN across api(53s)/events/migrate/sanitize/shortdesc; `go build`+`go vet` clean; no production request-path reader of the dropped objects remains (grep-verified ŌĆö all refs are migration DDL/seed/comments/DefaultKinds struct); provider-gate OK; gofmt clean; knowledge entities.py compiles. **Dev-DB cutover NOT run** ŌĆö it's a deploy step; only the throwaway `loreweave_glossary_test` was migrated.
> - **­¤ö¼ /review-impl on G4 (this session) ŌĆö found + fixed a CRITICAL data-loss bug.** The cutover's `TRUNCATE glossary_entities CASCADE` was UNGUARDED; migrations re-run every service boot (execGuarded has no ledger), so every production restart after cutover would have wiped ALL entities + wiki. Tests never surfaced it (they want a clean slate). FIXED: the TRUNCATE now runs only while the OLD `glossary_entities_kind_id_fkey` (ŌåÆsystem_kinds) still exists ŌĆö i.e. exactly once per DB, pre-cutover. Regression: `TestGlossaryCutoverG4_GuardPreventsRepeatTruncate` (migrate pkg, ephemeral DB: cutover ŌåÆ seed entity ŌåÆ re-run cutover ŌåÆ entity SURVIVES + FK repointed). Other findings accept+documented: revision-restore would break on a pre-cutover snapshot (impossible under full-reset ŌĆö cutover cascades entity_revisions); extraction on an un-adopted book silently skips all entities (`D-GKA-EXTRACT-UNADOPTED-GUARD`, LOW). Writer audit otherwise clean (createEntity was the only miss, fixed in 222786b4).

> - **­¤ö¼ /review-impl ALSO found + FIXED a pre-existing G4 PROD TIME-BOMB (this session).** The migration chain leaked a `system_kinds` `pg_attribute` slot **per run**: base `Up` did `ALTER TABLE system_kinds ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS genre_tags` (re-add) while G4e `DROP COLUMN genre_tags` removed it ŌĆö Postgres never reclaims dropped-column slots without a table rewrite, so each service boot leaked one slot toward the 1600-column ceiling (after which the service can't start). It had already exhausted the shared test DB (1590 dropped slots ŌåÆ blocked the events+migrate test packages). **FIX (`migrate.go`):** `genre_tags` FULLY RETIRED from the additive chain ŌĆö removed from `system_kinds` CREATE + the re-add ALTER + the `unknown`-kind seed + `Seed()` insert, and from `user_kinds` CREATE; G4e's `DROP COLUMN ŌĆ” IF EXISTS` kept as a ONE-TIME legacy cleanup (no longer fights a re-add). `genre_tags` was write-only mid-chain (only `Seed` wrote it; `SeedGenreKindAttr` derives `system_kind_genres` from `DefaultKinds` in Go, never read the column; handler `genre_tags` are output aliases from `system_kind_genres`). **PROVEN:** repeated full-suite migration runs now hold the dropped-slot count steady at 1590 (no growth) and the full suite is green; a FRESH DB from the fixed code carries 0 dead slots.

The collapse is `createEntity`'s value-seeding `SELECT DISTINCT ON (ba.code) ŌĆ” WHERE ba.kind_id=$1` ŌĆö it keeps ONE attr per code (universal-preferred), so a keep-both conflict gets one value row. New `frontend/src/features/standards/` (MVC; reuses glossary `tieringApi`/`tieringTypes`/`TierChip`/`FieldTypeBadge`). **M1** route+sidebar+`StandardsShell`(Genres/Kinds/Attributes)+`useUserStandards` (merged System+User, **User shadows System by code** ŌåÆ a cloned code = one editable USER row; `cloneGenre`/`cloneKind` via `clone_from_*`, shares useStandards keys so a clone refreshes the adopt pick-list). **M2** AttributesPanel (user-kind├Śuser-genre picker ŌåÆ user attrs + system parents' attrs read-only + create attach-by-code via `AttributeFormModal`) + kindŌåögenre link editing (`KindGenresModal`/`useKindGenres`, replace-set). **M3** create-from-scratch + edit (`StandardFormModal`) + soft-delete for genres/kinds/attrs + recycle bin (`TrashDrawer`/`useStandardsTrash` restore/purge). `tieringApi` += `createUserKind`/`patch`/`delete` + 6 recycle-bin methods (no new contract ŌĆö all G2 routes). `standards` i18n namespace ├Ś4 + `nav.standards` ├Ś4 (full parity). **VERIFY:** vitest **20/20** standards (merge-shadow, clone ids, attr form value/options parsing, link seed+save, std-form genre-vs-kind, trash list/restore/purge); full suite **2007 passed**, the 18 failures are pre-existing `D-I18N-PREEXISTING-DRIFT` (composition/world parity) + `@dnd-kit/core` missing-dep composition suites ŌĆö untouched namespaces, **0 regressions**; tsc clean (11 pre-existing composition only); i18n standards+common parity clean. **LIVE click-through (rebuilt FE image, gateway :5174, test account):** `/standards` renders 7 SYS genres + 13 SYS kinds; clone Fantasy genre + Character kind ŌåÆ both flip **SYSŌåÆUSER** with Edit/Delete; Attributes tab ŌåÆ pick Character├ŚFantasy ŌåÆ create "Bloodline" (code auto-derived `bloodline`, USER, attach-by-code through the gateway) ŌåÆ delete ŌåÆ gone. Proves the whole surface end-to-end. **System-tier writes stay out** (`D-GKA-SYSTEM-TIER-ADMIN`, blocked on admin-identity).
> - **Ō£ģ SYSTEM-TIER-ADMIN + ADMIN CMS DONE + LIVE-SMOKED (`D-GKA-SYSTEM-TIER-ADMIN` + `D-GKA-SYNC-HASH-ON-ADMIN-EDIT`, 2026-06-20; commits `3d1763e3` Lane A, `f90cb1fc` Lane B+C; pushed).** The admin write surface for the platform System tier + a separate thin CMS to drive it. Built fan-out (BE myself for the auth boundary; 2 background agents scaffolded+wired the CMS). **Lane A ŌĆö glossary admin API (`3d1763e3`):** `requireAdminScope` verifies an RS256 admin JWT via shared `contracts/adminjwt` (`ADMIN_JWT_PUBLIC_KEY_PEM`, base64-or-PEM), scope `admin:write`; POST/PATCH/DELETE for `system-genres`/`system-kinds`/`system-attributes-admin` with `content_hash` recompute (clears `D-GKA-SYNC-HASH-ON-ADMIN-EDIT`); `universal`/`unknown` guarded un-deletable. A valid HS256 USER token is rejected (RS256-only) ŌåÆ a regular user can never mutate System tier. Unset key ŌåÆ 503 fail-closed. Test: `TestSystemTierAdmin_*` (CRUD, 401 user-token, 403 scope, 503 unconfigured, hash recompute). **Lane B ŌĆö auth admin-session exchange (`f90cb1fc`):** `POST /v1/admin/session` ŌĆö a logged-in user who is an ACTIVE `admin_principal` SELF-mints an RS256 admin JWT (subject from their own token, authority only from the DB row; non-admin 403, disabled 404, rate-limited, audited). New `authjwt.LocalKeySigner` = production-safe in-process RSA signer for DEV/self-hosted (no KMS); `main.go` selects KMSŌåÆlocalŌåÆdisabled; `AdminIssuanceEnabled` now true for either path. Gateway proxies `/v1/admin`ŌåÆauth. Tests `TestAdminSession_*`. **Lane C ŌĆö `cms-frontend/` (NEW, separate from `frontend/`):** Vite+React+TS+Tailwind thin admin UI on :5175ŌåÆgateway `/v1`; login does the admin-session exchange + stores the admin token; Genres/Kinds/Attributes CRUD panels wired to the admin endpoints (13 vitest; tsc+build green). **infra:** compose passes admin keys through (disabled by defaultŌåÆfail-closed) + a `cms-frontend` service; `gen-admin-dev-keys.ps1` mints a dev keypair; auth+glossary Dockerfiles now `COPY contracts/adminjwt`. **LIVE-SMOKED** (`admin-cms-live-smoke.ps1`, full stack, dev local-RSA ŌĆö auth signs + glossary verifies, **kid matched** `bd90034eŌĆ”`): login ŌåÆ `/v1/admin/session` (role=admin) ŌåÆ System genre CREATE/PATCH/DELETE through the gateway; plain USER token ŌåÆ 401; non-admin ŌåÆ 403. **/review-impl (auth boundary, no HIGH/MED):** user-token bypass impossible (RS256 vs HS256, proven), no self-escalation (authority from `admin_principals` only), scope-gated, key-identity-pinned (kid/iss/aud/exp), fail-closed everywhere. LOW/accept: adminSession doesn't check session-revocation (consistent with every access-token endpoint), break_glass not distinguished (pre-existing R3), admin token in CMS localStorage (15m TTL, re-exchanged on 401). **For a persistent dev CMS:** run `infra/gen-admin-dev-keys.ps1` once, export the two vars, `docker compose up -d --build auth-service glossary-service api-gateway-bff cms-frontend`, then seed an `admin_principals` row for your user. **Remaining GKA debt:** `D-GKA-G4-SEED-CLEANUP` ŌĆö now CLEARED by the migration-ledger bullet below.

Ōłź F2 shared helpers (codeŌåÆid resolvers, base-version 409, read-tool rename `list_kinds`ŌåÆ`list_system_standards` + new `book_ontology_read`) Ōłź F3 reusable FE confirm-card. Audit: `docs/raid/evidence/neo4j-orphan-sweep/2026-06-18-orphan-audit.txt`. **Net: found a real bug, fixed it, live-proved it, AND reclaimed 2342 orphaned nodes ŌĆö net debt shrinks.** Fresh throwaway 1-ch campaign run (createdŌåÆstartedŌåÆcancelledŌåÆfully cleaned up, 0 residue): **S3b backoff** (knowledge `benchmark_missing` 409 ŌåÆ saga re-dispatched at exact 5s intervals, attempts=3) + **cancel** (runningŌåÆcancelling). **Remaining ~7 need a benchmark-FIXTURED extraction run (Neo4j build+cleanup) or are non-deterministic/browser:** cancel-prop-to-a-running-child (this run 409'd pre-job so `knowledge_job_id` was NULL ŌĆö nothing to propagate; needs the K17.9 fixture), rerank-byok, S2 idempotency, S3a governor, S5a estimate (NULL ŌĆö confirm not-recorded-vs-gap), breaker-pause (non-deterministic), S5c+S6 (browser). The saga demonstrably completes E2E (multiple `completed` campaigns exist).
> - **Ō£ģ `D-JOBS-P4-RETRY-COMPOSITION` SHIPPED (2026-06-17, /loom L + /review-impl, design [`ŌĆ”composition-retry-design.md`](../specs/2026-06-17-composition-retry-design.md)).** The earlier "bifurcated ŌåÆ not clean" triage was REFINED: retryability is **per-job** (worker-drivable Ō¤║ reconstructable), not per-kind (composition `kind` = the free-form `operation`, identical for the worker + inline paths). **Design realized with NO migration** via the existing `job_projection.params` JSONB: composition `create` emits `params.retryable = is_worker_drivable(operation, input)` (a job is worker-drivable iff the server stamped a recognized `input.worker_op`); jobs-service `derive_control_caps(status, kind, *, retryable=None)` offers RETRY on failed when `kind Ōłł _RETRYABLE_KINDS OR retryable is True`; both read sites (`routers/jobs.py` `_with_caps` + `control_job` gate + `sse.py`) pass the flag. composition `internal_job_control` routes `retry` ŌåÆ `_retry_generation_job_core`: 404-not-owned ŌåÆ 409-unless-failed ŌåÆ 409-not-worker-drivable (re-checked on the row) ŌåÆ 409-worker-disabled ŌåÆ re-submit a NEW pending job (copies `input`, `idempotency_key=None`, **guarded create for BOTH chapter-draft writers `chapter_generate`+`stitch_chapter`**) ŌåÆ enqueue. **/review-impl: 2 MED fixed** ŌĆö (1) drivability was over-broad via an `operation` fallback (inline stitch / user `operation="generate"` falsely retryable ŌåÆ instant re-fail); tightened to the server-set `worker_op` only + stamped `worker_op` on the stitch (engine.py) + decompose (plan.py) worker inputs; (2) stitch retry bypassed the chapter in-flight guard ŌåÆ now guarded. FE/SDK ZERO change (Retry data-driven off `control_caps`). VERIFY: jobs-service 29 (caps incl. TestClient read-API wiring) ┬Ę composition 95 (retry/emit/worker/plan/engine). **Retry now complete for translation + extraction + video_gen + composition.** Deferred: `D-JOBS-P4-RETRY-COMPOSITION-SSE-CAPS` (LOW ŌĆö a freshly-failed composition job's LIVE SSE frame omits the retry cap since the failed emit carries `params=None`; read API authoritative, refetch/reconnect recovers it) + `D-JOBS-P4-RETRY-COMPOSITION-LIVE-SMOKE` (needs worker stack + a deterministically-failing composition job). Lore stays the only un-wired retry (`D-JOBS-P4-RETRY-LORE` ŌĆö sync in-process ŌåÆ async-decouple refactor).

(4) **`D-SECONDARY-KIND-CONTROL`** (after rebuild) ŌĆö glossary_extraction cancelŌåÆcancelling+emitŌåÆprojection; wiki_gen resumeŌåÆpending (re-enqueue, no 500)+emit; wiki_gen cancelŌåÆcancelled. **(5) `D-P5-M3` (REAL extraction)** ŌĆö 2 projects on a published 5-ch book (bge-m3+Qwen2.5-7B, started via gateway API; passing-benchmark row inserted as fixturing for the orthogonal K17.9 gate), `P5_OWNER_CAP` temp-lowered to 1: `ZCARD ŌĆ”:inflight:{owner}` pinned at **1 (= cap)** for 24s, job A held the slot ($0.004), job B continuously deferred at **$0** (gate runs before try_spend). Key finding: a decoupled job submits ONE chunk/poll then waits ([runner.py:1948](../../services/worker-ai/app/runner.py#L1948)), so the per-owner cap only bites across CONCURRENT jobs ŌĆö what the smoke had to create. **(6) `D-P5-M2` (multi-owner)** ŌĆö with owner1 at cap, seeded 2 running jobs for a 2nd owner (own BYOK + book; rows direct-inserted as running ŌĆö worker poll/acquire path is identical): `inflight:{owner1}=1` + `inflight:{owner2}=1` + `total=2` simultaneously ŌåÆ owner2 acquired its OWN slot despite owner1 saturated, owner2's 2nd job independently deferred. Per-owner ZSET key makes isolation structural. **(7) `D-JOBS-P3-KNOWLEDGE-CANCEL-SUCCESS`** ŌĆö cancelled the inflight job A via the public cancel endpoint ŌåÆ 200 cancelled; `extraction_jobs`ŌåÆcancelled; `job.cancelled` outbox (H1 same-tx) ŌåÆ relay ŌåÆ `job_projection = knowledge|extraction|cancelled`. All synthetic data cleaned (4 jobs/projects, 2 benchmarks, projection rows, 151 Neo4j nodes); `P5_OWNER_CAP` restored to 5.
>   - **Ō£ģ `D-JOBS-SECONDARY-KIND-CONTROL` CLEARED (2026-06-17, this commit)** ŌĆö unified control now works for the secondary kinds. `kind` rides the control forward body (`forward_control`+`jobs.py`; other services ignore it ŌĆö Pydantic `extra='ignore'`, verified no `forbid` anywhere). `derive_control_caps` gives each its NATIVE caps: glossary_extraction/glossary_translation = cancel-only (pending|running); wiki_gen = cancel(pending|paused)+resume(paused), NO running-cancel (D-WIKI-M7B); `book_import` stays view-only (no control endpoint). translation `control_job` ŌåÆ `_cancel_secondary_core` (owner-scoped, 404/409, UPDATEŌåÆcancelling+emit on extraction_jobs/glossary_translation_jobs by a fixed kindŌåÆtable map); knowledge `control_extraction_job` ŌåÆ `_control_wiki_gen_job` (owner re-check via repo.get, repo.cancel/resume which emit). No FE change (caps flow through the SSE event; JobControls already renders per-cap). **/review-impl: 1 HIGH fixed** ŌĆö the unified wiki resume only flipped pausedŌåÆpending but did NOT re-enqueue (the consumer is event-driven ŌåÆ job stuck pending until a process-restart drain); fixed to mirror the native `resume_wiki_gen_job` (`enqueue_wiki_gen(_redis(), job_id)` after `repo.resume`), +2 tests (resume re-enqueues, cancel doesn't). > - **Ō£ģ M2.1 SHIPPED (translation-service only).** `segment_translations` table (per chapter/lang/segment; `source_content_hash` at translate-time, UNIQUE(chapter,lang,seg) + idx); [`workers/segment_status.py`](../../services/translation-service/app/workers/segment_status.py) `record_segment_translations` (full-chapter translate upserts all segments) + `compute_segment_status` (current vs recorded ŌåÆ `dirty`/`translated`); finalize hook `_record_segment_status` (best-effort post-commit; records against EXISTING segments, only fetches+builds if none ŌĆö no hot-path book-service call); internal + public `GET ŌĆ”/chapters/{id}/segments/status?target_language=` (public = book-VIEW, leak-safe). **VERIFY:** 717 pytest (+ real-PG recordŌåÆeditŌåÆdirtyŌåÆre-record cycle; DDL applied to dev PG :5555). **/review-impl:** MED-1 (per-chapter book-service fetch + finalize-time rebuild would falsely mark a mid-translation edit as translated ŌåÆ record-existing-first, fetch only if absent) + MED-2 (real best-effort swallow untested ŌåÆ +3 hook tests) FIXED.
>   - **Dirty semantics (doc):** `dirty` = the segment's SOURCE changed since last translate (or never translated for this lang). NOT glossary-staleness (that's `is_glossary_stale`, M3) and NOT translation-quality (a fallback-to-original block still reads "translated").
> - **Ō£ģ M2.2 SHIPPED (translation-service only).** Dirty-only re-translate via the existing job machinery. `CreateJobPayload`+`translation_jobs`+job/coordinator message gain `block_index_filter`+`seed_version_id` (additive migration). Worker `_process_chapter` block path: a partial branch (`_partial_retranslate_blocks`) translates ONLY the dirty blocks and overlays them onto the seed version's body (unchanged blocks copied verbatim), finalizing a normal new llm version ŌĆö **human-guard free** via `_PROMOTE_ACTIVE_SQL`. New `POST /v1/translation/chapters/{id}/retranslate-dirty` (book EDIT): dirty segments ŌåÆ block range, seed = latest llm version, enqueues a single-chapter `force_retranslate` job. **Guards:** seed must align 1:1 with current source else full-re-translate fallback; all-OOR filter ŌåÆ seed copy, no LLM spend; total-failure guard preserved. **VERIFY:** 727 pytest (overlay incl. dup/unsorted/mismatch/empty; endpoint dirty-union + 409 no-dirty/no-seed; seed chapter-scope; `_block_plain_text`); provider-gate clean; DDL applied to dev PG :5555. **/review-impl: HIGH-1** (seed loaded with no chapter scoping ŌåÆ cross-chapter seed IDOR via public create-job fields ŌåÆ `_load_seed_blocks` now `WHERE id=$1 AND chapter_id=$2`) **FIXED**; **+3 user-requested hardenings:** public `create_job` strips `block_index_filter`/`seed_version_id` (endpoint-only), partial path honors the pipeline's v3 verifier (injected `translate_blocks_fn`, not hardcoded v2), full-chapter memo from the merged body. **ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ `segment_index`/block positions assume stable structure** ŌĆö overlay falls back to full re-translate on any count mismatch; orphan `segment_translations` rows (segment-count shrink) are invisible to `compute` but a cleanup is owed.
> - **­¤ö© M3 IN PROGRESS (FULL A+B+C+D, plan [`...t2-m3-segment-matrix.md`](../plans/2026-06-15-translation-panel-t2-m3-segment-matrix.md)).** Risk-boundary commits: **M3.1 (A coverage) Ō£ģ** + **M3.2 (D per-segment glossary staleness) ŌÅ│** + **M3.3 (B drill-down FE + C limit fix) ŌÅ│**.
>   - **Ō£ģ M3.1:** `GET /v1/translation/books/{id}/segment-coverage?target_language=` (book VIEW) ŌåÆ per-chapter `{segment_total, translated_count, dirty_count, stale_count(0 till M3.2), needs_count}`. Lang-scoped book_chapters Ōŗł chapter_segments Ōŗł segment_translations. VERIFY: 731 pytest (+ real-PG count test: 3 segs/1 dirty, untranslatedŌåÆall dirty, foreign-langŌåÆempty).
>   - **Ō£ģ M3.2 (D):** `segment_glossary_usage` table (per-segment entity refs, language-independent; populated best-effort at finalize by scanning segment_text vs the book glossary source terms ŌĆö `_record_segment_glossary_usage`) + `segment_translations.is_glossary_stale` (reset false on re-record) + glossary_consumer per-segment flag (precise entity path joins segment_glossary_usage; coarse path stays chapter-level) + `stale`/`needs` in segment status + `stale_count`/`needs_count` in coverage + retranslate-dirty acts on `needs` (dirty Ōł¬ stale). VERIFY: 735 pytest (+ real-PG glossary-staleness cycle: usageŌåÆentity_updated flags only the using segmentŌåÆstatus staleŌåÆre-record clears; scan_glossary_usage pure). **Known LOW:** finalize re-fetches the glossary + scans per segment (perf-deferrable); usage scan capped at the endpoint's top-200 entities; substring term match mirrors build_glossary_context. (/review-impl follow-up: the entity set is language-INDEPENDENT ŌĆö the endpoint LEFT-JOINs the target translation ŌĆö so no last-language-wins drift; usage fetch bumped to max_entries=200 + INSERTs batched.)

> **­¤ö© SHARED `<ChapterListBrowser>` (B1) ŌĆö foundation DONE; full 7-site migration is an OVER-FIT (2026-06-14, pushed).** RAID-unblock re-checked: C22ŌåÆC28 are all composition/creation-flywheel; no remaining cycle touches a chapter-list call-site; `features/composition/*` isn't a call-site; `ChapterEditorPage` has no remaining brief + no uncommitted edits ŌåÆ **all call-sites RAID-free.**
> - **Shipped:** BE `editorial_status` (draft/published+pubrev gate) + `q` (title/filename ILIKE, 256-cap) on public `GET /v1/books/{id}/chapters` (`c963e466`, live-smoked: published 5 / draft 0 / bogus 400 / q='ń¼¼1' 1). `<ChapterListBrowser>` (`frontend/src/components/shared/`) ŌĆö server-paged (useServerPagedList+Pager), modes none/single/multi, debounced search, selection Set persists across pages + "select all N matching" loop-fetch, filters server-side. tsc 0 + vitest 5. **First consumer:** ExtractionWizard **pick** mode (`116470cf`) ŌĆö fixes the pick-cap (couldn't select past the first page).
> - **Per-site fit analysis (why NOT a clean 7-site swap ŌĆö the recorded B1 over-fit lesson, confirmed per-site):**
>   - `ChaptersTab` ŌĆö **already paginated** (offset + `<Pagination>`) with a **richer DataTable** (language/status/updated/actions). Migrating = downgrade ŌåÆ **skip.**
>   - `ReaderPage` TOC + `ChapterEditorPage` nav ŌĆö need the **full ordered chapter list in memory** for prev/next + reading-progress, not a paginated UI. Their cap fix is "loop-fetch all", NOT this browser. **Misfit.**
>   - `TranslationTab` ŌĆö chapters├Ślanguages **matrix** (2D grid), not a flat list. **Misfit.**
>   - `ContextPicker` ŌĆö **multi-book** picker; single-book browser doesn't fit directly. **Won't-migrate** (see cleared note below).
>   - `ChapterRangeStep` (campaign) ŌĆö range-by-`sort_order` predicate (from/to numeric inputs + a count), not a list browser. **Won't-migrate** (see cleared note below). (StepBatchConfig **all/range** were fixed in /review-impl ŌĆö see below.)
>   - **Ō£ģ `D-CHAPTERBROWSER-RANGE-MODE` + `D-CHAPTERBROWSER-CONTEXTPICKER` CLEARED (2026-06-14, won't-fit + real-bug-fixed).** Per-site analysis confirmed neither is a `<ChapterListBrowser>` fit, so the "reusable range mode" / "per-book browser" component work is dropped as over-fit (the B1 anti-pattern). The one genuine harm behind RANGE-MODE ŌĆö campaign `ChapterRangeStep` fetched `limit:5000`ŌåÆclamped 100, so the displayed "N published / N selected" **count under-reported** on >100-ch books (the campaign scope itself is a server-side `chapter_from/to` predicate, never the fetched list ŌåÆ no scope bug) ŌĆö was fixed **in place** with a loop-fetch (no new component). `ContextPicker` requests `limit:100` per book (at the clamp, no silent-20 bug); first-100/book is acceptable for a chat context picker and a per-book browser is the wrong shape (it's a multi-book grouped picker) ŌåÆ won't-fix.
> - **/review-impl fixes (this turn):** **#1/#2** ChapterListBrowser now resets page on a filter-prop change (render-time guard, not an effect) + shows a jump-to-first on an out-of-range page (was a false "No chapters"). **#3** StepBatchConfig now **loop-fetches the full chapter list** so `all`/`range` no longer silently truncate to 100 on big books (the default-select-all bug). **#4** added tests (select-all-N loop-fetch, Pager page-change, jump-to-first). **#6** memoized the pick Set. **#5 accepted** (pick mode double-fetches: the loop-fetch is required by the default `all` mode; the browser's extra paged fetch is trivial). tsc 0; vitest 69.

> - **Migration** `UpGlossarySearch` ([`internal/migrate/entity_search.go`](../../services/glossary-service/internal/migrate/entity_search.go)) ŌĆö `pg_trgm` + GIN trigram indexes on `cached_name`, `glossary_aliases_text(cached_aliases)` (IMMUTABLE wrapper so the aliases expr can be indexed ŌĆö PG18 treats bare `array_to_string` as non-immutable), and `attribute_translations.value`. Wired in main.go after UpEntityRevisions.

Sort-by-`cached_name` (indexed). **Counts-sort DEFERRED** per DESIGN REVIEW ┬¦3.5 (correlated subqueries ŌåÆ expensive at 20K; needs denorm counters).

Each hit carries a `match` payload `{field_code, snippet, highlights}` with **rune-offset** highlights (pure `buildEntityMatch`/`computeEntityHighlight`, ported from `book-service/search.go`). Simple mode unchanged (no `match`).
> - **Migration-deadlock fix** (`execGuarded`, migrate.go) ŌĆö wrapped the DDL migrations in a transaction-scoped advisory lock (`pg_advisory_xact_lock`). Adding the new migration to the shared test harness exposed a **pre-existing** cross-package `migrate.Up` race (parallel `go test ./...` packages migrating the shared dev DB ŌåÆ 40P01). The lock serializes concurrent migration DDL (uncontended/instant at prod startup). **Residual:** migration-DDL-vs-test-DML contention across packages is pre-existing parallel-suite flakiness (the unrelated `grantclient BaseURL` flake also surfaces under parallel runs) ŌåÆ **run the glossary integration suite per-package or with `-p 1`**.

> **Ō£ģ B1 (PARTIAL) ŌĆö shared pagination primitives (M, one /loom, 2026-06-14).** User opted into building the deferred shared-chapter-browser epic NOW but scoped to the two non-RAID call-sites (chapter import review + translator modal). > **Ō£ģ TRANSLATOR WIZARD ŌĆö smart chapter selection + large-book support (L, one /loom, 2026-06-14).** The `TranslateModal` requested `listChapters(limit:200)` ŌåÆ book-service `parseLimitOffset` returned the default 20 (not a clamp) for `limit>100` ŌåÆ wizard showed only 20 of N chapters; it also pre-selected ALL with no per-chapter status, so "2000 translated + 10 new" gave no way to target the 10. **Shipped:** (1) **BE clamp** `server.go parseLimitOffset` `limit>100ŌåÆ100` (was fallback-to-20) ŌĆö fixes every chapter-list consumer (closes the B0 half of the deferred shared-browser epic). (2) **`coverageClassify.ts`** (new lib, 11 unit tests) ŌĆö classifies each chapter for the target lang as `untranslated/translated/stale/failed/running` from the coverage matrix; `needsIds` = `untranslated Ōł¬ stale Ōł¬ failed`. (3) **`TranslateModal` rewrite** ŌĆö on open fetches ALL active chapters (paged loop limit:100) + `getBookCoverage`, shows a **summary header** (total ┬Ę per-status counts), a primary **"Translate N that need it"** (submits the needs-set, `force=false` ŌĆö the idempotency gate skips fresh-completed), a **paginated** (100/pg, Prev/Next) classified list with per-row **status badge** + checkbox + **quick-select chips** (Needs/Untranslated/Stale/Failed/All/None); keeps the V3 verifier + `force_retranslate` toggle. Default selection on open / language-change = the needs-set (via callback, not useEffect). **VERIFY:** go build+vet 0, FE `tsc` 0, vitest **14** (11 lib + 3 modal); **live smoke** (rebuilt book-service, 105-ch book): `limit=200ŌåÆ100` (was 20), `limit=50ŌåÆ50`, `offset=100ŌåÆ5`. **/review-impl: MED-1** (`pending` queued chapters were classed untranslated ŌåÆ the primary button would dup-submit; the gate only skips `completed` not pending/running) **+ LOW-1** (`fetchAllChapters` total-fallback could truncate to 1 page) **FIXED**; verified the coverage endpoint has **no pagination cap** (full accuracy on 2000+ ch). Accept/doc: the "All"/manual chips can still submit in-flight chapters with force=false (deliberate manual override, pre-existing). **Deferred B1 (shared `<ChapterListBrowser>`) stays open** ŌĆö this was a focused TranslateModal optimization, not the cross-call-site component.

> **Ō£ģ CHAPTER IMPORT UPGRADE ŌĆö folder/large import (L, one /loom, 2026-06-14).** The old `ImportDialog` only took multi-file (no folder), didn't sort (FileList order ŌēĀ numeric ŌåÆ jumbled chapters), had no review, and uploaded `.txt` ONE FILE PER REQUEST (4232 files = 4232 requests = unusable). **Shipped:** (1) folder picker (`webkitdirectory`) + files picker + drag-drop, `.txt`ŌåÆbulk path / `.docx`/`.epub`ŌåÆexisting async job; (2) **natural sort** by leading numeric filename prefix (`parseChapters.ts` `naturalCompare`, unit-tested); (3) **paginated review** (`ChapterImportReview.tsx`, page-through Prev/Next + jump, 50/pg, handles 4232) with per-row order#, **inline title edit**, size, **exclude** checkbox + select-all; title previewed client-side from the `ń¼¼Nń½Ā` header (BE re-derives, FE edit = override); files read with a progress bar; (4) **bulk endpoint** `POST /v1/books/{id}/chapters/bulk` (`bulkCreateChapters`, book-service import.go) ŌĆö array of `{original_filename, content, title?}`, sequential FE batches of 100 ŌåÆ monotonic `sort_order` (max+1) preserves order; title override else `extractChapterTitle` regex; publishes canon (parity w/ single import); **idempotent re-import** (skips existing `original_filename` ŌåÆ resume-safe on partial failure), returns `{chapters_created, skipped_existing}`. **VERIFY:** go build/vet 0, FE tsc 0 + vitest 8 (sort/title/filter); **live HTTP smoke** on rebuilt book-service via gateway ŌĆö order 1/2/3, `ń¼¼Nń½Ā` title parse + override, and idempotent re-import (2 created ŌåÆ re-run 1 created / 2 skipped, total 3 no dupes). **/review-impl: MED-1 idempotency** (re-import duplicated on retry) **FIXED** (filename-skip). Tradeoffs: bulk = one scene-less chapter/file (no structural scene-split; draft body is canonical); `original_language='auto'` (matches single import). **GAP:** book-service `internal/api` has **no test harness** (0 test files) ŌĆö covered by live smoke; building one is separate infra.
>
> **Deferred `D-C16-NULL-WORK-ROUTE` (RAID C16 ŌåÆ C17, naturally-next-phase):** C16 makes `POST /work` survive a knowledge-service outage by persisting a Work with `project_id=NULL` + a backfill marker (writer not wall-blocked at setup), and the packer tolerates a null project_id ŌåÆ empty grounding (deployed-path live-proven). BUT every generate/grounding/outline route is keyed on `{project_id}` in the URL (and `outline_node.project_id` is NOT NULL), so a STILL-PENDING null-project Work is not yet addressable for end-to-end Generate ŌĆö it becomes fully draftable only after the **backfill** stamps a real project_id (live-proven: retry on recovery ŌåÆ project stamped on the same row). Closing the "draft against a still-pending Work" gap needs an id-keyed Work-resolution route + outline-on-null handling = FE+routing work, owned by **C17 (writer flow polish / guided first-run)**. C16's locked scope is setup-resilience + backfill, which is met. (Adversary BLOCKER reclassified to this row ŌĆö it is a scope boundary, not an in-scope defect; derivative-guard / 4xx-discrimination / backfill / migration all reviewed CLEAN.) **C17 UPDATE (2026-06-14):** C17 was FE-only and built the **happy path** (knowledge UP ŌåÆ real project_id ŌåÆ guided first-run). It guards the null-project_id case in FE (the guided setup `onSuccess` only auto-creates the first scene when `created.project_id` is non-null) but did NOT add the id-keyed BE route / null-outline handling ŌĆö that needs a BE schema+route change, OUT of an FE-only cycle. **D-C16-NULL-WORK-ROUTE stays OPEN** (BE id-route + outline-on-null), now targetable at a future BE cycle. Also flagged this session: a **pre-existing unrelated working-tree build break in `frontend/src/pages/book-tabs/TranslateModal.tsx`** (duplicate `pageCount`/`safePage` from an incomplete `usePagedList` refactor) ŌĆö blocks Vite HMR; NOT a C17 file, NOT staged in the C17 commit; needs a 1-line dedup next session.
>
> **Deferred ŌĆö shared chapter-list browser + `limit>100ŌåÆ20` bug:** `docs/specs/2026-06-14-shared-chapter-list-browser-epic.md`. `book-service parseLimitOffset` returns the DEFAULT 20 (not a clamp) for `limit>100`, so TranslateModal(200)/TranslationTab(200)/ExtractionWizard(500)/ChapterEditorPage(200)/campaign ChapterRangeStep(5000) all silently get **20 chapters** ŌĆö the "translator shows only 20" report. Fix = a 1-line clamp (B0) + a paginated `<ChapterListBrowser>` (B1) reused across ~8 call sites. **B0 SHIPPED** (2026-06-14 ŌĆö `parseLimitOffset` now clamps to 100). **B1 PAGINATION HALF SHIPPED** (2026-06-14 ŌĆö `components/pagination/{usePagedList,Pager}` extracted + adopted by import-review + translator-modal). **B1 remainder still deferred** (the full `<ChapterListBrowser>` with selection-modes/filters/select-all-N across the RAID-owned call-sites ŌĆö composition/* + ChapterEditorPage). The import flow above is separate (client-side file list, not the server browser).

> **Ō£ģ GLOSSARY BULK-ACTIVATE (M, one /loom, 2026-06-14).** Closes the latent trap from the translation work: extracted entities default to `status='draft'` and the translation-glossary query only returns `status='active'`, so a freshly-extracted book translates WITHOUT glossary until entities are activated ŌĆö and there was no bulk way to activate them. **Shipped:** BE `POST /v1/glossary/books/{id}/entities/bulk-status` (`bulkSetEntityStatus`, entity_handler.go) ŌĆö one UPDATE over `entity_id = ANY($::uuid[])`, book-scoped + `deleted_at` guard + edit-grant, validates statusŌłł{active,inactive,draft}, drops malformed ids, caps 1000; **no outbox** (status isn't in the wiki-staleness payload). FE multi-select in `GlossaryTab` ŌĆö per-row checkbox + select-all (loaded) + Gmail-style **"select all N matching"** loop-fetch (so activation isn't capped at the 100-row page) + FloatingActionBar Activate/Deactivate; `glossaryApi.bulkSetStatus`. i18n ├Ś4. **VERIFY:** glossary BE 5 real-DB tests (activate-book-scoped, deactivate, bad-status 422, empty 400, no-auth 401), FE tsc 0 + vitest 70, go vet 0, provider-gate clean. **/review-impl: MED-1** (select-all silently capped at loaded 100 ŌåÆ added select-all-N loop-fetch + action targets full selection) **+ LOW-1** (added inactive test) **FIXED**; verified pgx `[]uuid.UUID`ŌåÆ`uuid[]` encoding + cross-book isolation + chi route precedence live. Usage: Glossary ŌåÆ filter status=Draft ŌåÆ select all (ŌåÆ "select all N") ŌåÆ Activate. **NOTE:** the underlying GlossaryTab list still has no pagination UI (hard 100-row view); "select all N" works around it for bulk-status but browsing >100 is a separate pre-existing gap.

> **Ō£ģ TRANSLATION PUBLISH/VERIFIER UX + AUTO-PROMOTE (L, one /loom, 2026-06-14).** Root-caused a "Vietnamese translation is terrible" report (book `019eb60eŌĆ”`): the glossary layer was fine ŌĆö the user was reading an **old qwen2.5-7b** version (15ŌĆō41% leftover Han, wrong names) while a clean **gemma-4-26b** re-translation sat **unpublished**, because interactive single re-translation used `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` (only campaigns promoted) AND the publish UI was an **orphaned route** (no nav from the workspace). Investigation + plan: [`docs/specs/2026-06-14-translation-publish-verifier-ux-investigation.md`](../specs/2026-06-14-translation-publish-verifier-ux-investigation.md), [`docs/plans/2026-06-14-translation-publish-verifier-ux.md`](../plans/2026-06-14-translation-publish-verifier-ux.md). **Shipped:** (1) **auto-promote** ŌĆö `chapter_worker.py` `_PROMOTE_ACTIVE_SQL` (extracted constant) now promotes a clean (`unresolved_high_count=0`) re-translation over an existing active version for **both** campaign + interactive, guarded so it **never clobbers a human edit** (`authored_by='human'` subquery in the `ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE WHERE`); campaign + interactive paths converged. (2) **TranslateModal** verifier-model + qa_depth + max_qa_rounds (sends `pipeline_version='v3'` when enabled, since verify only runs in v3) + `force_retranslate` toggle (else the idempotency gate skips already-done chapters). (3) **TranslationTab** translated cells are now clickable ŌåÆ `/books/:bookId/chapters/:chapterId/translations?lang=` (publish UI reachable). (4) **data fix:** book `019eb60eŌĆ”` ch1 active ŌåÆ gemma v4. i18n: 8 locale files (books+translation ├Ś en/vi/ja/zh-TW). **VERIFY:** translation **683** ┬Ę FE tsc 0 + vitest **27**; **live smoke:** `_PROMOTE_ACTIVE_SQL` executed on real PG (`test_promote_active_pg.py`, all 4 branches: insert / llm-promote / human-skip / M5b-flag-skip ŌĆö ran live, not skipped). **/review-impl: MED-1** (modal advanced state not reset on open ŌåÆ stray force-retranslate) **+ LOW-1** (stale jobs.py skip-gate comment) **+ LOW-2** (guard had only SQL-string coverage ŌåÆ added real-PG test + extracted SQL constant) **+ bug #5** (ChapterTranslationsPage's re-translate button was dead ŌåÆ wired TranslateModal scoped to the chapter) **all FIXED**. Accept/doc: a human-*published* (not edited) llm version is still overwritten by auto-promote ŌĆö matches the PO's "auto-promote except human-edited" choice.

> **­¤¦Ą /warp PARALLEL WORKFLOW MODE (2026-06-12) ŌĆö built + /review-impl'd + dry-run-validated; COMMITTED.** A new opt-in workflow mode: decompose a task into **provably-disjoint slices**, fan them out as `Agent(isolation:worktree)` sub-agents, reconcile at a **human-gated** junction. Fills the empty 2├Ś2 cell (parallel + human-gated + lightweight; RAID is parallel-but-autonomous, loom/amaw are serial). **Spec** [`docs/specs/2026-06-12-warp-parallel-mode.md`](../specs/2026-06-12-warp-parallel-mode.md) (positioning ┬Ę TRIAGE 2-stage gate ┬Ę Slice Manifest + machine-checkable disjointness ┬Ę reuse-from-RAID/amaw/loom map). **Built:** `.claude/commands/warp.md` (skill) ┬Ę **safety spine** `scripts/warp/slice-manifest-validate.py` (pairwise path-prefix-disjoint write-sets + frozen-pin + reads-bounded) wired as gate verb **`python scripts/workflow-gate.py slices <manifest> [--verify-frozen]`** ┬Ę `scripts/warp/worktrees.py` (Python worktree lifecycle ŌĆö bash wrappers fail on this box) ┬Ę `scripts/warp/slice-runner-prompt.md` ┬Ę `docs/warp/EXAMPLE-manifest.yaml`. **81 tests.** **/review-impl found+fixed:** HIGH-1 (interior/partial globs gave FALSE-disjoint verdicts ŌĆö `services/*/budget/**` vs `services/campaign/budget/**` read CLEAR ŌåÆ now fail-closed) ┬Ę MED-2 (`_is_dirty` git-error ŌåÆ force-remove of uncommitted work) ┬Ę MED-3 (case-insensitive-FS false-disjoint) ┬Ę LOW-5 (CRLF parse). **Dry-run on a throwaway sandbox PASSED end-to-end** (2 isolated worktree agents ŌåÆ branches persisted + visible ŌåÆ `merge-tree` exit 0 with both files ŌåÆ worktrees.py cleanup; fully torn down). **Finding D1 (fixed):** `isolation:worktree` bases each slice on a COMMITTED ref (HEAD), never the orchestrator's uncommitted edits ŌåÆ `--verify-frozen` is now HEAD-based (BLOCKs an uncommitted frozen path) + warp.md requires commit-before-fan-out. **Ō£ģ `D-WARP-REAL-TASK-VALIDATION` EXERCISED (2026-06-13) ŌĆö surfaced + FIXED `D-WARP-WORKTREE-BASE-FLAKY`.** First real-task fan-out (`clear-defers-codeside`, 5 substantive code slices) FAILED: `Agent(isolation:worktree)` handed out **inconsistent bases** ŌĆö slices 1 & 5 landed on a stale `main` (66 commits behind the committed DESIGN HEAD `e2370823`, missing M1-M5), slices 3 & 4 on the correct HEAD. A slice silently built against missing code. RAID never hit this because `worktrees-create.sh` pins the base explicitly; warp delegated base-selection to the opaque harness. **Fix (committed):** Coordinator captures `BASE_SHA` after the DESIGN commit; every slice's **Step 0** self-heals via new `worktrees.py pin-base --branch <BRANCH> --base <BASE_SHA>` (`git checkout -B <branch> <sha>` ŌĆö immune to the harness base since all worktrees share the object store), unreachableŌåÆ`BLOCKED(base_mismatch)`; Coordinator re-verifies each DONE slice with `merge-base --is-ancestor`. **Proven against real git: `scripts/test_warp_base_pin.py` + 16 worktrees unit + 64 manifest-validate green.** warp.md + slice-runner-prompt.md + spec ┬¦10 updated.

**Ō£ģ FULL REAL-TASK RE-RUN COMPLETE (2026-06-13) ŌĆö slices 2-5 cleared + reconciled; surfaced + FIXED a 2nd warp bug.** Re-ran `/warp` fan-out (base `dbc419a9`); **all 4 slices pinned correctly to base (1 commit each, descended-from verified)** ŌĆö the base-flakiness is gone. Reconcile = clean linear cherry-pick (ZERO conflicts, the disjointness dividend) ŌåÆ **per-service suites green post-merge: lore-enrichment 884 ┬Ę worker-ai 228 ┬Ę translation 682 ┬Ę chat 342 ┬Ę provider-registry go build+vet OK.** Slice commits on feat: `8a801fe5` (s2 lore-enrichment race/sweeper/poison) ┬Ę `a49911af` (s3 worker-ai SKIP-LOCKED; D-WX-RUN-SAMPLE-DECOUPLE was already done at base) ┬Ę `edb9e551` (s4 translation flag-coupling/shell-tests/block-obs) ┬Ę `feeec92c` (s5 composer-substream obs + cancel-log noise). **Cleared:** D-M2-COMPOSE-TASK-{RACE,SWEEPER,POISON} ┬Ę D-WX-TRIO-FANIN-RACE ┬Ę D-2B-{DECOUPLE-FLAG-COUPLING,SHELL-UNIT-TESTS,T3A-BLOCK-CHUNK-ROWS} ┬Ę D-M3-COMPOSER-SUBSTREAM-OBSERVABILITY ┬Ę D-CANCEL-FINALIZE-LOG-NOISE. **2nd warp bug found+fixed ŌĆö `D-WARP-PINBASE-PRIMARY-CLOBBER`:** `pin-base`'s `git checkout -B` mutates whichever worktree the cwd resolves to; slice 2's isolation worktree was never created so it ran in the PRIMARY checkout ŌåÆ yanked the main repo into detached-HEAD (recovered: salvaged the commit to its branch ref, restored feat). Fix: pin-base now REFUSES in the primary worktree (`--absolute-git-dir == --git-common-dir` ŌåÆ exit 4 `wrong_worktree` ŌåÆ slice returns BLOCKED), regression-tested (`test_pin_base_refuses_in_primary_worktree`). Warp test suite = **86 green**. `/warp` is now real-task-validated end-to-end.
